It can be played using the MELEE and WIZARD rules -- or you can use TFT: IN THE LABYRINTH, which comprises MELEE, WIZARD, and much more. This booklet will set up the rooms for you, give you decisions to make, and tell you the outcomes of those decisions. You will need to supply six-sided dice, a straightedge, scratch paper, and either MELEE and WIZARD or TFT: IN THE LABYRINTH, ADVANCED MELEE and ADVANCED WIZARD. Each room (by a lucky coincidence) is the size of a MELEE map -- or you can build the same shape out of TFT megahexes. DEATH TEST II can be played in several ways: Solitaire - One lone player can take both sides, creating a party of adventurers and running them through. This booklet will give instructions regarding the foes in each room. Two-Player - One player creates a party of adventurers and enters the labyrinth. When foes are encountered, the other player controls them. Multi-player - Each player creates their own character with a Game Master running the adventure from the book."

Name: Operation: Rapidstrike
Description: An introductory level adventure module for TSR's Top Secret game, Operation: Rapidstrike (code name: Popeye) was originally written as a tournament scenario for GenCon XIII. The published version followed and pits 5 - 7 players against Madamoiselle Larreau as they aim to rescue Nobel prize-winning chemist Dr. Felix Fendelmann and prevent Mlle. Larreau's use of a new "mind-expanding" drug.

Name: DragonQuest Gamesmaster's Screen
Description: From the back of the GM Screen... The DragonQuest Gamesmaster's Screen provides the gamesmaster with privacy and instant access to DragonQuest's most frequently consulted charts and tables. For the players, there is a copy of the Weapons Chart and associated charts and summaries. User submission: The screen is printed on cardstock in full color on 4 fold-out panels.

Name: Runemasters
Description: Strong NPCs and their use in Runequest From the back cover. Here are 45 Rune Lords, Rune Priests, and Rune Lord-Priests of the 15 cults surveyed in Cults of Prax. Each Character is completely individualized, experienced, equipped with spells, treasures, bound spirits, and so on. But William R. Keyes' book is much more than a collection of statistics. He also discusses how Rune Masters should be created and about the logic of their growth, how each type of RuneMaster should be played, what their retinue (if any) shoulde be, how a referee can organise his NPCs with coherent melee tactics and team strategy, etc.

Name: Knights and Berserkers and Legerdemain (2nd Ed.)
Description: Adventure! Worlds of Wonder Wealth Power From the back of the box: Why do you play role playing games? Probably the reason I play, to turn fantasies into an acceptable reality, and the key word is REALISM! Compare realisms. Compare KABAL to your present system and see!

Name: Warlock's Menagerie
Description: Cover says: The Warlock Menagerie A Supplement for Fantasy Games. Especially "The Complete Warlock" The third and final book of The Complete Warlock series. This book focused on monsters.

Name: Dungeon Masters Adventure Log
Description: From the front cover: Here's the ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS DUNGEON MASTERS ADVENTURE LOG, the second playing aid designed specifically for the DM of ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS™! No longer need the DM rely on memory and sketchy notes to keep track of one's players in the midst of play. Now all the information needed about the adventurers and their exploits can be kept handy in front of the DM at all times. The DUNGEON MASTERS ADVENTURE LOG contains two different sheets (shown on the back cover). The first type is for recording information about the adventurers, their hit points, armor class, magic items and die roll adjustments. The second provides information on the marching order of the party, the monsters they encounter, treasures they recover, and any unusual events that occur. In addition, this book contains many important tables in an easy reference form, including tables for experience point awards, encounter reactions, movement, surprise, listening, and a new table of collected combat modifiers. If you find this product useful, you should look into our line of other helpful aids, and look for the TSR logo on upcoming publications from the Game Wizards.

Name: RuneQuest (2nd Edition)
Description: From the back of the box: RuneQuest is an easy-to-play game of Fantasy Role-Playing. Participants create imaginary characters to go adventuring through a world of fantasy where magic is real, heroism the accepted norm, and virtue is rewarded! Characters begin as commoners or young noblemen and work or fight their way through trouble and adversity to reach greatness, collecting gold, magic, and fame along the way. They can develop as formidable fighters, clever magicians, solemn priests, alchemists, thieves, scholars, or all of them at once! There is no limit to what a character can do.

Name: I.S.P.M.V. Tethys
Description: From front: An entire Starship drawn in 15mm scale for use with miniatures. The TETHYS was constructed by Captain Van Tromp at P.E.L. shipyards in 116.23. Captain Van Tromp hires his ship out to organizations requiring the capability of transporting a full company of fighting men. Missions for the TETHYS include anything from planetary exploration to planetary invasion.

Name: Basic Role-Playing
Description: From the back cover: BASIC ROLE-PLAYING Battleboard, Chit-Dice, & Character Pieces Illustrated Many Examples Solo Play or with Several Players Character Forms Supplied Easy to Learn The Fundamentals of Role-Playing BE FANTASY ADVENTURING IN 30 MINUTES! User summary: A simplified 16-page version of RuneQuest. Many later Chaosium games were based upon this system.

Name: Traveller Dice Set
Description: From a 1984 Gamescience product catalog: 5/8th-inch, six-sided black dice with a distinctive red numeral on white starburst design, approved for and designed especially for use with TRAVELLER™ by GDW.

Name: Deities & Demigods
Description: From the back cover (of 1st to 3rd printings): Deities & Demigods Cyclopedia Provides the Dungeon Master with Gods, Heroes and Monsters from Myth, Fiction and Legend for use in rounding out an Advanced D&D campaign. Within this book are seventeen pantheons of divinities, each profusely illustrated. Also included are new material on Clerics' conduct and their relationship with their Deities, information on Character mortality and immortality and more!

Name: Wilderlands of the Fantastic Reaches
Description: The fourth and final supplement in Judges Guild's Wilderness series. It details the villages, castles, citadels, lairs, islands, ruins, and terrain in a section of the Wilderlands of High Fantasy campaign setting. It consists of two 16-page booklets (one 32-page book in later printings) and two double-sided 17" x 22" maps: Campaign Map #15: Isles of the Dawn Campaign Map #16: Southern Reaches Campaign Map #17: Silver Skein Isles Campaign Map #18: Ghinor Highlands

Name: Monster and Treasure Assortment Sets One - Three
Description: This resource, is subtitled Sets One-Three: Levels One-Nine. All crunch and no fluff, it packs 900 monsters and 900 treasures into 33 pages of lists. 100 monster encounters and 100 treasures per level. Each monster or treasure is listed in abbreviated stats form and fills one to two lines of text.

Name: Death Heart
Description: An overland adventure and dungeon set in the Arduin universe. Contained 8 new monsters, 8 new magic items, and several unique traps. From the introduction: "This module is designed to tie together the three previous modules plus add a new dungeon to the mythos. For this reason there are overland maps, and a large number of specific and random overland encounters. The map is so designed as to allow it to fit easily into anyone's world or to give those of you who desire it a new, playable world of your own (one of the so-called "middle kingdoms" or a part of one of them). It's entire up to you."

Name: The Iron Wind
Description: From inside the front cover: "This is the first of the Loremaster Series, a group of fantasy aids designed to each provide the background for a complete campaign of any level. Laid out for your players on the four-color map are no less than 5 dungeons and 3 cities through which they may search for riches or death. But worlds are not all towers and dungeons, so the Loremaster series provides the cultural and political background for a series of wilderness adventures as the players journey across the map learning about their world. Two maps (scale 1" = 20 miles) are provided, one labeled with suggested locations for each city or dungeon, so that you can release to the players just the information their characters might actually have. The dungeons and citadels are shown in sinister perspectives and laid out with numbered, keyed, floorplans - ready for you, as Gamemaster, to people them with creatures that will provide an appropriate challenge for your players. A second volume in this series is currently in development to allow you to widen the scope of your player's adventures and more will be forthcoming. Also available (by Spring '81) is a full-color continental map done in the same style as the back cover of this booklet. Ten million square miles of forests, savannas, steppes, deserts, mountains, islands plains and lakes on a 24" x 36" poster-sized map. Look for it at your hobby store."

Name: Inferno
Description: From the author's Foreword: "Inferno is based primarily upon the poem "Inferno" by Dante and is also fitted as a scenario into my campaign, Nidevellir. My purpose in writing it is twofold -- first, to answer the perennial question of Judge's when the party finds a cursed scroll: "GO DIRECTLY TO HELL!!" now what do I do? And secondly, to provide a power base to the evil immortals in the campaign. There may arise occasions in which an irate Cleric will geas some poor fighter into returning something that is on the Devil's plane and this allows the character somewhere to go for the adventure."

Name: The Temple to Athena
Description: from back cover: The fanatical worship of the goddess of all was interfered with by Desar, the ultimate evil ...and as the temple ages and deteriorates the state of things becomes more uncertain. Evil magic and devout clerics exert their efforts to control the party of adventurers! Adaptable to any fantasy role playing system.

Name: The Palace of Ontoncle: DragonQuest Adventure One
Description: From the back of the book: The Palace of Ontoncle is a fantasy role-playing adventure scenario that can be used in conjunction with SPI's DragonQuest. The gamesmaster uses this booklet to describe the missions for the players and to guide them through the palace of an evil magacian and his henchman.

Name: Sewers of Oblivion
Description: From the back cover: "This solitaire takes place beneath the City of Terrors, but is not directly connected with that solitaire adventure. Sewers of Oblivion is not for novice adventurers - instead characters up to 7th level, with as many as 425 combat adds, will find a challenge or two within. However, less mighty characters (150-300 combat adds) will find a more exciting adventure and greater rewards in the end."

Name: Bushido (2nd Edition)
Description: This was the second version of the game that went on to be published by F.G.U. under the same title. It is largely similar to the first version published by Tyr, but includes a complete upgrade of the text including all errata.

Name: The Castle Perilous
Description: User summary - An RPG system published in the early days of the hobby. Based on the use of three D6 dice for resolution. For those who don't have enough 6 sided dice, a table of random numbers is included. This books contains the complete rules and two sample adventures.

Name: The Third Planet from Altair
Description: Reader summary When extraterrestrial signals arrive on Earth, it's off into outer space to determine their origins at the source. Who - or what - sent them? What do they mean? You'll find the answers at the Altair system in this interstellar romp. The Third Planet from Altair marks the first appearance of recurring character Dr. Nera Vivaldi.

Name: The Morrow Project
Description: This is the core rule book for The Morrow Project game setting. From the back of the UK 2nd edition rulebook The year is 1989. As the civilized countries of the world exchange gifts of nuclear missiles, the small volunteer population of the Morrow Project sleep far away from the blossoming nuclear fireballs in a deep dreamless winter. These few people, long prepared for the holocaust which rages around them, await the time when their ever-watchful machines will awaken them. They expect the time of their cold sleep to be a mere three or four years but everything had gone well right up to the war and it was now that the infamous gremlin Murphy shifted the gears. The "cryonauts" sleep on for one hundred and fifty years before their delinquent machines finally restore them. One hundred and fifty years of eroding culture and technology; one hundred and fifty years of fall-out boosted mutations; one hundred and fifty years of dog-eat-dog survival. In the Morrow Project you will take the role of one of these "cryonauts", and by doing so place yourself in a situation of forced survival. You know nothing of the changeling world around you and find dangers around every turn. You will find yourself face-to-face with the conditions created after a nuclear war. Genetic mutations in almost every form of affected wildlife constantly surprise and endanger you; radioactive "hot spots" are an always present peril; and you find that the most dangerous animal left on Earth continues to be Mankind itself. Survival is the only rule left to a chaotic society. Somehow the separated Morrow Teams must contact one another, find their main base, and again bring civilization to the human race. They are Man's last hope for a future beyond survival, but mankind and the blasted forces of nature seem to unite in the task of destroying that last hope. Can the Morrow Teams survive in the world of 2139? CAN YOU SURVIVE???

Name: The Rogues Gallery (AD&D 1e)
Description: From the front cover: No longer will you the Dungeon Master need to spend precious time laboring over the task of generating nonplayer characters. This valuable booklet contains hundreds of pre-rolled non-player characters of all classes and types, complete with alignments, sex, personalities and much more. THE ROGUES GALLERY is specially designed to be compatible with ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS™. If will save you rime in creating your non-player characters, thus allowing you the time to pursue the other interesting aspects of your campaign.

Name: The Toughest Dungeon in the World
Description: Excerpted from the Introduction: "This adventure is designed for the Tunnels and Trolls role-playing system. It is literally an adventure involving Trolls in Tunnels. In order to play you will need pencil, paper, a 20-sided die, several six-sided dice, and the 5th edition of the Tunnels & Trolls rules. Most adventures are designed for the "good guys", those brave adventurers who invade subterranean lairs in search of loot and thrills. This one, on the contrary, tries to get you to look at things from the "monster's" point of view. After playing a Troll for a while, you will begin to realize that the delvers are the true monsters of the story."

Name: Darthanon Queen
Description: Science-Fiction adventure in the Ley Sector From the Introduction: All (3) scenarios in this adventure take place on board the Darthanon Queen, a standard Type M subsidized Merchant currently in the service of the Val'Tor Lines, on the Jumhuriyah to Gheelis run. Includes a random monster generator for the Dyson hiding on board.

Name: City of Lei Tabor
Description: A Runequest Gateway (non-gloranthan) product. From the front cover. Complete fantastic Runequest city. All inhabitants, shops, cults, temples, inns, politics, thieves, treasures, adventures, 96 pages.

Name: The Book of Treasure Maps II
Description: From the front cover: "FIVE scenarios, complete adventures with judges and players maps, full background & accompanying rumors. Ready to add to any campaign."

Name: Top Secret Box Set (First Edition)
Description: User Summary TSR's take on Bond and the super spy genre. The box set includes a 68 page rule book outlining character creation, equipment, and combat and a 32 page introductory module entitled "Operation: Sprechenhaltestelle - Code Name: Pisces" along with 2 20-sided dice. Character creation consists of 6 primary stats (Physical Strength, Charm, Willpower, Courage, Knowledge, and Coordination) and 8 derived stats (Offense, Deception, Evasion, Deactivation, Life level, Movement Value, Hand-to-Hand Combat Value, and Surprise Value). Characters are further detailed with skills in specific knowledge areas. Combat resolution is handled by dice and modified by situations. The rules include specific handling for boxing, knife fighting, and martial arts as well as hit location and effects.

Name: Plunder
Description: RuneQuest book of Treasure. From the back cover. this book contains 640 pre-generated treasure hoards and 43 magical treasures for Glorantha for use in any RuneQuest campaign.

Name: The Blade of Allectus: DragonQuest Adventure Two
Description: From the cover: A philosopher king and his beautiful daughter inhabit a lonely island stalked by a fearsome curse and rich in hidden treasures. You and your party sail to a dangerous challenge in this great adventure from the world of DragonQuest.

Name: High Guard (Second Edition)
Description: From the back of the book (2nd edition): At last, extensive rules for the personnel and starships of the interstellar navies of the universe. Detailed character generation for naval characters, including the academy, and medical school, and 5 new skills. Rules for starship design, construction, and combat, with tonnages ranging to one million tons! High Guard, created for the interstellar naval adventurer and referee. Be sure to look for Book 4, Mercenary, with its coverage of the army, the marines, and of military adventures. User Summary: This book covers Imperial naval characters and ships of the Imperial navy in the Traveller setting.

Name: Arms Law (1st Edition)
Description: Arms Law, 1st Edition was the first Rolemaster product released by Iron Crown Enterprises when it was published in 1980. Arms Law is the core rules weapons system for Iron Crown's original Rolemaster fantasy role-playing system. This First Edition book was later combined with the natural animal and martial art attack charts from the book "Claw Law" as the Second Edition book "Arms Law & Claw Law". Arms Law covers weapon attacks and includes a 12 to 24 page book and several pages of separate detailed attack charts for 30 weapons and statistics for various critical strikes and fumbles. Note: Internet sources state the first printing had a blue-and-white illustrated cover, single-sided attack charts, and 24 pages of text. Later versions had a black-and-white illustrated cover and were printed in a smaller font, reducing the number of pages to 12.

Name: Lightning Class Cruisers
Description: Lightning Class Cruisers is a detailed treatment of the background, specifications, capabilities, and interior layout of the 60,000 ton Azhanti High Lightning class interstellar cruisers for the Traveller RPG. Although this supplement was distributed as part of the Azhanti High Lightning board game, it was information for the Traveller RPG. It was never sold separately by GDW but is frequently found separated from its parent game in collections and often can be found sold independently of the original game by those that sell used games. It is not necessary to play the original game.

Name: GrailQuest
Description: user comment MicroQuest #3 in the Metagaming MicroQuests series, this is a programmed adventure for Melee and Wizard, and suitable for Advanced Melee and Advanced Wizard. The box includes a rulebook with 13 pages of rules and 19 pages for the programmed adventure. Also included is a sheet of counters that you cut apart yourself, and a d6. From the back of the box: "Dare you seek the Holy Grail.... The Knights of the Round Table are one of mythology's shining examples of sacrifice and honor. When King Arthur calls on the heroes of Camelot to go forth and seek the Last Supper's Holy Grail, you are there. You go forth with them on Christendom's greatest quest. He who finds the blessed Grail must be of purity, honor and courage. There is no adventure more worthy of chivalry's finest flower, no greater way to grace. GrailQuest is a programmed fantasy adventure that can be played solitaire. You are one of King Arthur's Knights of the Round Table. You create your character, equip him with armor and weapons, and send him forth on the great quest. Monsters, danger and death await, traits of flesh and spirit the seeker must endure."

Name: Dungeon Geomorphs Set One to Three
Description: From the front cover: This new booklet is a combination of the original three sets of Dungeon Geomorphs released by TSR The Game Wards: Basic Dungeon, Caves & Caverns, and Lower Dungeons. It contains 30 square geomorphic pieces and 15 rectangular semi-geomorphic pieces that are ready to cut out and use. With only 4 of the 45 units contained in this product, Dungeon Masters can form hundreds of thousands of maps in mere seconds! No longer will a Dungeon Master be forced to spend hours upon hours drawing a dungeon level that plays through in one session - with these geomorphs DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Fantasy Adventure Game players can look forward to infinite variety even though they may play more often.

Name: Ley Sector
Description: Ley Sector is a supplement for Traveller. A 32 page guidebook includes 16 subsector maps describing 411 planets, encounter tables, rumors, and news. Also included is a 22" x 34" full-color sector map with 8 planetary maps on the reverse side. Note that Ley Sector was redefined in GDW's The Atlas of the Imperium.

Name: Security Station
Description: user comment This is MicroQuest #5 in the Metagaming MicroQuests series of programmed adventures for Melee and Wizard. It has a light science-fiction setting. From the back of the box (minus several spelling errors): "Jas N'orn was Cidri's most renowned collector of the weird and bizarre. He had the most, yet wanted more.... Mnoren gates to a ruined Earth gave Jas his chance: nuclear genocide had rained down on the world's green hills. Burrowing in the rubble, Jas' searchers found a functioning remnant. Its technology was strange and its secrets dangerous. Jas knew he must have it. Such was the way that the mystery and death of the Security Station came to Cidri's past. With the Mnoren gone, it now kills on alone. Security Station is a programmed fantasy adventure that can be played solitaire or by a group. You become a treasure seeker in a menacing relic of Earth's atomic demise. Great secrets and immense wealth await the brave invaders of this strange place. Create and equip your character and with a group of hardy friends, you will step into the pulsating curtain of multi-colored lights. There is no turning back, an adventure of fear and intrigue has begun."

Name: DragonQuest Boxed Set (First Edition)
Description: SPI's foray into the fantasy role-playing market in 1980. Only available as a boxed set, the set included: 3 Rulebooks Character Generation, Combat Magic Skills, Monsters, Adventure 1 17"x22" Tactical Display 1 sheet of 100 die-cut counters 1 sheet of 20 die-cut counters (randomizer chits) May have been replaced with 2D10 (d%) in some sets

Name: Beasts, Men & Gods (1st Edition)
Description: The rulebook for the Beasts, Men & Gods RPG.

Name: City State of the World Emperor
Description: Another detailed fortified city in Judges Guild's Wilderlands campaign world. This one is known as Viridistan, the City of Spices, or the Immortal City. This campaign supplement is comprised of three booklets and two maps. Booklets: Guidebook Map 6: a 48-page book detailing the lands around the city state (villages, ruins, religions, cultures, politics, etc.) Shops: an 80-page book of all the shops within the city state City: an 80-page book detailing all of the bars & taverns in the city state, available rumors, military layout, etc. Maps: Campaign Map 6: two 17" x 22" maps (Judge, Player) for the section of the Wilderlands of High Fantasy that contain the city state Viridistan (Map): a 34" x 22" map of the city state

Name: Library Data (A-M)
Description: Supplement 8, Library Data (A-M), is a supplement for Traveller. This 48 page booklet includes sections on The Imperium, interstellar society, psionics, chronology, library data entries, the Solomani, megacorporations, the Emperor's List, and a map of the Imperium.

Name: Scouts & Assassins (First Edition)
Description: Scouts & Assassins includes an advanced character generation system for the Scout Service, an alternate system for early discharge from any career when a survival roll is failed, and a basic character generation system for Assassins. It also includes an 11"x17" deck plan of the Serpent Class Type "S" Scoutship in 15mm scale, along with a four page booklet describing the ship class and the company that built it. The book's Introduction is a fairly glowing letter from Marc W. Miller, informing all "Fellow Travellers" of Dan Rapp's great adventures, and how his work is enjoyed by several of the Workshop's design staff. Scouts & Assassins, 1st Edition First Printing, January 1980 Second Printing, March 1980 Third Printing, December 1980

Name: The Arduin Adventure
Description: Sold as a rulebook or as a boxed set of the same rulebook with character sheets and magic item cards. From the back cover: This game is an introduction to the world of adventure gaming, and may be learned in less than an hour by anyone age twelve and older. If you are not afraid of trying something new, you will discover a game that is exciting and different from any game you have tried before. The Arduin Adventure provides the novice with a set of simple and understandable guidelines for learning the basic concepts of Role Playing. Role-playing is the heart of all adventure gaming systems now on the market. Once learned these concepts allow the player to easily understand them. The Arduin Adventure has a unique modular learning system that permits the gamer to apply any part of it to another system, or part of another system to itself. Although already a complete game, this system is infinitely expandable with the only limit being the player's imagination. So read on and enter the world of action and adventure. It awaits only your participation to come alive!

Name: Legendary Duck Tower and Other Tales
Description: Official Playing Aid for Runequest Gateway From the front cover. A complete campaign adventure pack Long-ruined city and deserted citadel Tales of fabulous riches and terrible monsters Rumors of horrible dooms and powerful magic

Name: Overkill (2nd edition)
Description: From the back cover: "Out of the north swept the hordes of Lerotra'hh, the Death Goddess of Khazan. Led by veterans of the Khazan-Ranger wars, the Kazani troops over-whelmed the token resistance in the south until they came to the keep of Marionarsis, near the southern end of the mountain pass known as the Gap in the Teeth of the Gods. The vetrans call Marionarsis a wizard-butcher and necromancer - and they call his castle OVERKILL Your character is one of the Khazani Death Host, and is asked to perform a secret mission of destruction inside the castle called Overkill. This dungeon is Number 7 in the series of Solitaire Adventures designed for play with Tunnels & Trolls. You will need the rules to T&T, a few six-sided dice, paper, pencil, and this booklet, to play. This edition of Overkill has been completely re-written and revised by Michael Stackpole from the preciously published dungeon of the same name by Mike Brines."

Name: The Maghrib
Description: From the front cover: Wyrdworld II. An adventure for 8 to 15 characters of levels 7 through 12. Set in Wyrdworld. With adaptations for AD&D and Variant Systems. Playing time is from 4 to 6 hours. From the introduction: Heliarchus II, Emperor of Ilchania has called the party together to act as an escort and guard for his nephew Imhadrek, who is going to Asfar, the City of the Jinn to collect tribute from Malik Katsham, king of the Jinn. The duties of the party are to guard Imhadrek and the tribute, and to make sure they both return in good condition. User note: The production value for this item is very low: 10 xeroxed sheets of paper stapled together.

Name: Player Character Record Sheets
Description: For the Dungeons & Dragons Fantasy Adventure Game. From the cover: "Do you have trouble keeping track of your dwarf's hit points? Are you constantly checking to see what your thief's abilities are? Did your magic-user miss going up a level because you lost the piece of paper with all of his treasure on it? Well cast your gaze on this item. At last, all that valuable information has been put on one sheet of paper."

Name: Space and Beyond
Description: user summary The fourth book in the Choose Your Own adventure series sends the reader deep into outer space. Interstellar quests and encounters abound, and author R.A. Montgomery adds some philosophical musings to the package.

Name: Tower of Ulission
Description: First of a two-part adventure in which players must journey overland to find the dead village of Ulission and confront its Black Tower.

Name: Your Code Name Is Jonah
Description: reader summary After covering genres such as science fiction, horror, mystery and a Western, choose Your Own Adventure sets its sights on espionage in the sixth entry in the series. The Cold War was still going strong in 1980, when this book was penned, and Soviet spies abound to make the reader's path difficult.

Name: Across the Bright Face / Mission on Mithril
Description: Contains the Adventures 'Across the Bright Face' and 'Mission on Mithril' in a flip-over format: one adventure starts from one side and if you flip the book upside down you can read through the other adventure. Has data on an All Terrain Vehicle in the middle.

Name: Escape from Astigar's Lair
Description: The players have one hour to escape the stronghold of the evil mage Astigar. This was the official AD&D tournament dungeon from Michicon VIII and the tournament scoring system used during the convention is included.

Name: Tancred
Description: Planet details for Ley Sector 1232. A 48-page book detailing the Tancred System Campaign setting, a planet in the Outreamer Subsector of the Lay Sector, where rebels battle an entrenched aristocracy. Included is a complete description of an entire civilized planet as well as other significant installations on other planets. The center fold contains a 11"x17" two-color map of the planet. Complete details are given for a B class starport. Four Special scenarios are included to get campaigns underway. Contains details of a mercenary regiment and 15mm-scale card stock cut-outs of their armored vehicles with 15mm scale interior details. Vehicles include: three Weapons Carriers, four Armored Personnel Carriers, two Jeeps, two scooters plus weapons turrets for all main vehicles including Tank turret, Artillery turret, Missile Turret, Special Weapons turret and smaller caliber automatic weapons."

Name: Duck Pond
Description: A Runequest Gateway (non-gloranthan) adventure. It does use cults from Cults of Prax From the back cover. A Runequest Game aid. A complete campaign adventure pack. Fishing & Trading village, Duck Pond, & of course, Frwack the duck, who controls the five elemental spirits. riches and doom await all who journey to Duck Pond.

Name: Shadows / Annic Nova
Description: From title page/introduction: The Traveller Adventure titled Shadows was used as the Traveller Tournament situation a WinterWar 1980. Substantial portions of the Traveller Adventure title Annic Nova originally appeared in issue number 1 of The Journal of the Travellers' Aid Society, which appeared in June, 1979. Issue number 1 is now out of print, and no longer available from the publisher. Material has been rewritten, and interior drawing have been re-executed for this edition.

Name: The Gateway Bestiary
Description: From the back of the book: Expand the horizons of your RuneQuest campaign with the exotic creatures in this book. All new, they can be used to create numerous interesting encounters. This book contains 99 new monsters from a variety of sources, presented in the clear and concise format of RuneQuest, complete with illustrations. This is the first of our Gateway products, which are designed to aid the referee who desires to run a RuneQuest campaign without the restrictions of the limited flora and fauna of Glorantha. GIANT ANTHROPODS - This section includes your favorite insects in human size forms such as giant ants, spiders, or wasps capable of carrying a rider. LEGENDARY BEINGS - Provides monsters from terrestrial mythos, such as the hydra, gorgon, lamia, or sphinx. CELTIC HORRORS - Contains terrifying creations of the Gaelic mind. These include kelpies, voughs, and more. H. P. LOVECRAFT CREATIONS - These are the otherworld nightmares of Lovecraftian mythos such as the rmi-go, nightgaunt, or shoggoth. NATURAL ANIMALS - This section fills the more obvious omissions of íhe original RuneQuest rules by including stats on frightening carnivores like lions or bears, and the smaller animals useful as familiars, such as owls or monkeys. DINOSAURS - Here are the giant prehistoric creatures... from the bulky sauropods that are likely to crush an adventuring party by accident to the terrifying carnosaurs which should make any batch of Rune level characters turn and run. MISCELLANEOUS TYPES - These include the whimsical creations of Lewis Carroll to the fatal anthropophage from a well-known science fiction movie. Complete statistics are provided, just roll them up and see the players try to figure out what they're up against!

Name: Dallas: The Television Role-Playing Game
Description: From the front of the box: "All the drama and power, the wheeling and dealing, the loves and lives of tv's greatest family are yours to enact in this fun-filled, fast-playing game." Boxed set includes: Rules of play - 16 pages (includes three ready-to-play Original Episode scripts: "The Great Claim," "Sweet Oil," and "Down along the Coast") Scriptwriter's Guide - 16 pages Major Characters - a booklet of 20 perforated pages: "Information for the Director", "Player's Rules Outline", 9 double-sided character sheets for the major characters 2 perforated sheets with 56 cards of minor characters 2 six-sided dice Game box

Name: All the Worlds' Monsters III
Description: From the back of the book: "Now! The long-wanted 3rd volume of the All the Worlds’ Monsters series is here-112 pages packed with monsters and play-aids. Included are 238 new monsters, an all-series index, a RuneQuest stats conversion essay by Steve Perrin, and grouping lists by monster type, level, and appearance. The stats were created by dungeon masters from across the United States and Canada. Each monster is rated for hit dice, armor class, movement, whether and how well it swims and flies, its intelligence range, dexterity, alignment, normal habitat, the probability of it being present in its lair. by a die roll for number present, the probability of the presence of treasure and its type, how it attacks, and a description of general appearance and special characteristics."

Name: Twilight's Peak
Description: This booklet is a comprehensive adventure for Traveller entitled Twilight's Peak. Set in the Spinward Marches, it deals with a search for the remains of a military expedition lost on a mission over a century ago.

Name: The Nine Doctrines of Darkness
Description: from the back cover: TOME OF EVIL It was the King's favorite wine-flask -- until the ages-old spell wore off. Now the black bound tex of evil doctrine is the center of attention for both good and evil beings. It is your goal, Mortal!

Name: Tollenkar's Lair
Description: Tollenkar's Lair is a ready-to-play labyrinth for use with The Fantasy Trip game system. "Landmaster Hall was destroyed 248 years ago. Nothing remains above ground but scattered rubble. The tunnel complex below remains...and has recently been put to sinister use..."

Name: Dungeon Tiles
Description: Generic floor tiles for Dungeon Adventuring in any game system. Brought to us by the same folks that did Star Fleet Battles. Came with four pages of rules governing movement, stacking, etc.

Name: Thieves' Guild II
Description: Thieves' Guild II is a supplement which includes information about expanding combat rules, expanding experience awards, and obtaining hirelings. The supplement also includes a new adventure.

Name: Arena of Death
Description: Heroic Combat in the Fantasy World of DragonQuest From the boxed version cover sheet: Arena of Death places players, who assume the roles of characters, in a life or death gladiatorial combat. Drawing its combat system from SPI's popular game, DragonQuest, Arena of Death is a stand alone adventure that is perfect for introducing the novice into the realm of fantasy role-playing. Players create their own characters, arm them, and send them into the arena to face other gladiators or monsters. The game system covers character generation, maneuver and martial action, grievous injury, and asking for mercy from the crowd. From the boxed version and Ares version rules: Introduction - The individual combats can be played as stand-alone games or they can be played as part of a complete campaign... Note - Arena of Death is a stand-alone adventure using the combat system from SPI's DragonQuest. As such, the game uses the counters and Tactical Display from that game. The combat system presented in Arena of Death can be adapted by using the provision of DragonQuest combat as an optional rule for this game.

Name: The Fantasy Cartographer's Field Book
Description: From the front cover: "Complete fantasy mapping system. Four different mapping grids. Special map key & record pages." This is a book of simple resources to aid gamemasters in mapping out fantasy scenarios and campaigns. A set of mapping symbology tailored to this purpose is included.

Name: Game Masters Shield and Reference Tables
Description: From one of the 3rd edition Inserts: Project Director's Screen (GA-1) The Project Director's Screen has the most important tables and charts for The Morrow Project printed on heavy cardboard. Everything the Project Director needs to run The Morrow Project combat system efficiently and effectively is included on the screen. Also included in this package are separate sheets with the statistics of the basic weapons of The Morrow Project as well as statistics for other weapons which the Project Director might need. These sheets are three-whole punched in order to allow the Project Director to keep all of his Morrow Project information in a binder. GA-1 has: 1 - 11" x 24" Screen and 3 - 8 1/2" x 11" inserts printed on heavy cardboard

Name: The Treasure Vaults of Lindoran
Description: From the Players' Introduction: "Your party has come through the wilderness and mountains surrounding Lindoran to seek after the fabulous treasures of an Elf-King now a thousand years dead. The protection spells have been weakened over time so that you think that you can get inside of the Vault safely. New problems have arisen, however, for now there is an ancient red Dragon in the Plaza outside of the double doors leading into the Treasury. The doors are made of four metals -- Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum -- and a like set of keys hand on the wall nearby. But first the Dragon must be gotten past... Perhaps he can be driven off, maybe he can be sneaked past and the keys stolen (for all of the many legends about this place being told in the Mannish areas far to the Southwest tell of magical metal keys which hold the power to enter certain sections of the Vaults) or maybe he can be bribed... What is your party going to do? The Dragon isn't going to be leaving, for he has a good-sized hoard taken from the sack of several Elven and Dwarven towns. Do you have enough men and magic to tackle this job? A lot of healing is going to be necessary, for rumors are that once the Vault is entered, it cannot be easily left; going out is harder than is coming in... Be brave, may Bes travel on your shoulder."

Name: Abyss
Description: From the back of the adventure: "ABYSS is second in a series of Pocket Adventures designed for solitaire play with the Tunnels & Trolls game system. To enter the Abyss, you'll need a few six-sided dice, paper, pencil, and the Tunnels & Trolls rulebook."

Name: Odysseus: Role Play for the Homeric Age
Description: From the introduction: Odysseus is a game of adventure in ancient Greece. It combines the fast-moving combat of Greek hand-to-hand warfare with the incredible mythology of Homer. The ancient Greeks are well known as the most advanced of philosophers. From the abstract to the concrete, they theorized, postulated, and experimented. From algebra to calculus, from creation to ethics, they were the best of thinkers. And yet, in this environment of intellectual enlightenment, they worshiped deities as diverse as they were complex. Imagine a young Greek warrior leaving home for adventure, fame, and fortune, seeking a place of honor among heroes such as Achilles, Heracles, and Agammemnon. Could he survive and reach his goal, or would he die in the trying? Only his skill, courage, luck, and the favor of the gods will lead to success. Odysseus uses a role-playing format to simulate the epic adventure of mythic Greece. The rules are composed of six sections, each describing an aspect of play, mechanics, or genre in the game, such as combat between individuals and armies, sieges, and naval engagements, as well as the people, cultures, gods, and monsters of the Hellenic world. Together they form the backbone of a role-playing game that attempts to be "realistic" in nature, while remaining enjoyable to all the players. May the gods favor your conquest!

Name: Deadwood City
Description: Tangle with the meanest man in the West! You ride into Deadwood City and before you can say, "Git along little dogie," Kurt Malloy and his gang of desperadoes are hot on your trail! (From back cover.)

Name: Encounters in the Corelian Quadrant
Description: Encounters in the Corelian Quadrant is the second in a series of starship books approved for use with Traveller™.

Name: Furioso
Description: from the back cover: This scenario is the complete Advanced Dungeons & Dragons tournament adventure used at the Denver Gamers Association convention, Ganghis Con held in May 1980. Centuries ago, in a far away place, an awesome weapon was invented that threatened to destroy the world. A great battle was fought over this weapon and as a result, it was tossed into the deepest part of the ocean. The weapon was thought destroyed and the world was safe once again. Many years passed, and rumors began circulating that religious fanatic named Cimosco was attempting to find the final resting place of this weapon of awesome power. If he managed to find and restore this weapon, its horror would once again be unleashed on the world. Cimsco searched for years and finally found the resting place of the terrible weapon. He enlisted the aid of the sea-dwellers and raised the remains of the artifact. What emerged from beneath the sea was nothing more than a coral-encrusted lump, but repeted blows with mighty sledges released the weapon from its stone like bondage. Four centuries of coral ans salt had not harmed it in any way. Until a way is found to destroy the weapon once and for all, its hellish power threatens all living things.

Name: Kahldath, the Messiah
Description: From the front cover: Wyrdworld adventure 1. An adventure for 10 to 20 characters of levels 7 through 9. Playing time is from 5 to 7 hours. From the introduction: Zhoral, the High-Priest of Ahriman in the city of Carzal has sent forth notice through all the lands of the Crimson King that he is seeking a party of strong-willed adventurers to take on a quest of an unusual nature, for which they will be amply rewarded. Those who are interested in this proposal should meet in the temple of Ahriman in Carzal, at the 15th hour on the first day of the month of the Striped Lizard. They will be expected to bring all of their equipment with them and to be prepared to embark the next morning. User note: The production value for this item is very low: 9 xeroxed sheets of paper stapled together.

Name: Pen-Latol's World
Description: From the Introduction: This role-playing game-aid was designed as an adventure for novice characters as part of a larger campaign or by using the pregenerated characters as an evening's entertainment. Pen-Latol's World is approved for use with G.D.W.'s Traveller. User summary: The setting is non-canon. Includes a large black and white world hex map usable in other games and pre-generated Traveller characters with a background rationale for being on this world.

Name: The Rescue of Ardene
Description: From the introduction: The evil wizard Gregorvich Brownski has captured Ardene, the beautiful elf princess of Silverwood. He holds her on the remote island of Laiah, deep underground. He has a standing challenge to all who dare, to try to rescue her. Official dungeon for American Eagles Tournament held 24 Feb 1980.

Name: Wizards 2001
Description: Figure Set for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: "This 25mm fantasy figure set is specifically created for the fantasy role playing gamer. ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS is a trademark owned and licensed by TSR Hobbies, Inc. The policy of GRENADIER MODELS is to constantly upgrade our product & we reserve the right to alter specifications and contents without prior notice. Figures 1980 by GRENADIER MODELS, INC." This set includes: A. Illusionist B. Druid C. Cleric D. Magician E. Druid w/ Dart F. Enchanter G. Warlock H. Wizard I. Sorcerer J. Archmage w/spell

Name: Merc - D.O.W.
Description: Publisher description: "Mercenaries, Dogs of War, a role-playing or miniatures game of late 20th century mercenaries. Rules include complete weapons lists, character generation, scenarios, maps, floor plans, which are excellent with or without a GM." This version of the core rules includes two rulebooks plus a number of stapled-in inserts consisting of maps, charts, and tables. The primary rulebook has a cover printed on green stock. The second rulebook, with a cover made of white stock, is marked inside as "Volume Two" and "Book Two." Comments in the rulebooks indicate that the second rulebook was created to add revised and updated rules that arose from further playtesting.

Name: Skinwalkers & Shapeshifters
Description: From the title-page: A Guide to LYCANTHROPY for Players, Dungeon Masters, and The Curious

Name: Mage
Description: MAGE is a a module of the Archaeron Games System designed to be used with most existing role-playing games as well as with the Archaeron games System itself. Other fantasy role playing rules can be used in conjunction with MAGE simply by converting the spell levels of those rules to MAGE Magic Levels or EL. The Endurance (EN) of a persona or character can be expressed in terms of 'hit points', 'fatigue points', etc. simply by dividing the total of 'hit points' by EN to yield the points fer EN level shich are lost. MAGE can also be used by players as a game in its own right as a contest between MAGICIANS. Source: Book introduction

Name: Imperial Form 1: Military Player Character Forms
Description: A set of 25 character sheets plus directions for use.

Name: RuneQuest Judges Shield
Description: Runequest Judges Shield is similar to the Dungeons & Dragons Judge's Shield. This two-color printed screen includes resistance tables, weapons tables, monster tables, and attack tables. Note: This item was published by Judges Guild and not Chaosium.

Name: Roaming Monsters - Level 2
Description: High quality metal figure set for use with DUNGEONS & DRAGONS and other fantasy role-playing games. Dungeon Dwellers are 25mm metal figures specially designed for use with Dungeons & Dragons and other fantasy role-playing games. They have been boxed in special assortment sets that give the player exactly the monsters and adventurers he needs for Basic D & D. All guesswork has been eliminated for the beginning player. Each Action Adventure box contains 18 to 22 figures suitable for the dungeon level indicated. All together, the sets provide every figure needed for Basic D & D - in useful quantities for gaming. This set, Roaming Monsters-Level 2, contains the following: - HOBGOBLINS - CARRION WORM - ORCS - GIANT TICKS & GIANT BEETLES - GNOMES - GHOULS/ZOMBIES (Source: From the back of the box)

Name: Pieces of Eight
Description: From the back cover: PIECES OF EIGHT expands upon the original rules for SKULL AND CROSSBONES and is intended for use with that game. Many of the additional rules sections are not hard and fast rules, but rather are recommendations and discussions of subjects for the Game Master. In many cases it is better for the Game Master to make rulings when many factors can enter into a ruling than to attempt to detail long and complicated rules to cover the almost limitless factors that can enter into a ruling. Some of the new rules sections, such as the rules on voodoo, are optional and are intended to be used by Game Masters with a liking for the fantasy element of fiction. Those familiar with Robert E. Howard's Black Vulmea will immediately understand how and when to apply these rules and recommendations. Finally, PIECES OF EIGHT includes three complete adventures plus outlines for further adventures. Also included with the adventures is a map of Port Royal (not the historical city but the simplified version from the designer's campaign) with a description of the buildings and some of the inhabitants. A similar map of a smaller city, Fort de France, the port city of Martinique is also included. These descriptions give the Game Master some ideas of how to construct and populate some of the other towns which are mentioned on the SKULL AND CROSSBONES map and, hopefully, will generate other adventures using them as a basis. Though we have said it before, it still warrants mention that no rules are cast in bronze and any of them can be changed by the Game Master to vary the adventures, but care should be taken when doing so.

Name: Sword of Hope
Description: Sequel to Tower of Ulission, the players must solve the Eldritch riddles which surrounded the Sword Wars of 1200 years ago and recover the Sword Myrlani and give life to she who was slain by the Sword of the Sightless Eye.

Name: Planetary Data Sheet
Description: From the cover sheet: This data sheet features an icosahedron map grid with hexagonal surface for use in mapping planetary surfaces. It also includes spaces for recording pertinent statistics about the planet. This form is especially useful for mapping, search or exploration scenarios. The Planetary Data Sheet is a valuable tool for any science-fiction role-playing game player – it can be used to expand the scope of any planetary surface encounter.

Name: Portals of Torsh
Description: "The first book in the "Portals" series, it details an area reachable only through the use of Teleportation devices called "Portals". It includes considerable History and Economic description of the area, one town of Lizardmen and another of Humans, and several adventures including a Lizard Wizard's Tower. Also included are Monster Lairs, Wilderness descriptions, Wandering Monster tables and major Character descriptions. Portals of Torsh can easily be fitted into any campaign, and is suitable for mid-level characters."

Name: The Tombs of Valla
Description: From publisher blurb: The third in a series of playing aids based on the world Dyon. It is one of three dungeond used at ProCon I during the summer of 1980. Over a year in the making this dungeon proved a challenge to all who entered.It was used in the 1st-3rd and 4-6th level categories. The other dungeon used at this level was Notnaidag Robber Headquarters also one of my designs.I hope that you who are playing this dungeon will have as much fun as the people at ProCon and the Stygian Wargaming club that play-tested it. Author John C. Clegg Tombs of Valla, 1st Printing 1980

Name: X. Tournament Combat
Description: 88. Basic Tactical Procedure for Quick Combat User Summary: DragonQuest 1st Edition and 2nd Edition both ended with rule 87. The play-tested, yet unpublished, supplement Arcane Wisdom began with rule 89. Rule 88 was missing. This one page supplement published by SPI in 1980 and included as an insert to the later shipments of DragonQuest Boxed Set (First Edition), and in some mailings of SPI's Ares #4, is that missing rule 88.

Name: Bifrost Volume 4
Description: From the introduction: This is Bifrost Volume IV. Within its pages you will find the means to ride into combat fully caparisoned on your black war-horse, or to swoop out of the sun astride a winged steed. Furthermore, there is a menagerie of fantastic creatures against which to match strength and skills. There are also some amendments and additions to the first three volumes.

Name: Foes
Description: A collection of pregenerated statistics for Intelligent Glorthan races. It includes over 1200 individuals with individualised weapons, spells and equipment including familiars and mounts. From the back of the book: The world portrayed in the role-playing game RuneQuest is filled with dangerous and exotic life forms. Some are legendary, such as the manticore, griffin, or minotaur. Some are unique to Glorantha such as the Dragonewt, morokanth, or broos. Some are traditional, such as the troll, elf, or dwarf. Some are from myths, such as the awesom dragon, the wyrm, or the terrifying wyvern. Many are human, including varous types of barbarians, peasants, mercenaries, and adventurers. This book, FOES, contains the statistical profiles of over 1200 such characters prepared for instant use by referees in hundreds of situations. Normal calculation of this many non-player-character statistics would take the average person more than 200 hours of work - yet FOES presents them at once, with variety, thought, and wit for less than 1 cent each.

Name: Chivalry & Sorcery Gamemaster's Shield
Description: Judges Guild's screens for the Chivalry & Sorcery RPG. The product consisted of two tri-fold cardstock screens printed on both sides.

Name: Weres: The Accursed
Description: This "mini-system" adds rules for playing shapeshifting were creatures in the Ysgarth Rules System. The rules can be used for player characters or non-player characters.

Name: Port Xanatath
Description: From the Introduction: Port Xanatath is another in an ever-improving series of Traveller™ play-aids. We are quite proud to present this adventure for your entertainment. The Pirate sanctuary in the following pages is not a place to conquer, but a place that players can go to for adventure. Port Xanatath is APPROVED FOR USE WITH TRAVELLER™ with references to Mercenary and High Guard. User summary: The setting is non-canon. Includes a large full color hex map.

Name: Grint
Description: From publisher blurb: "Het vreemde verhaal van het dorp Grint, waar Priesters, Druïden, Magiërs, Tempelridders, Zonneruiters en de Wolvenvrouwe wanhopig proberen het gevaar van de Dragers van de Zwarte Kroon te keren. Grint is een verhaal voor het systeem Queeste, en bevat 84 kaarten." translation: "The strange story of the Grint village, where Priests, Druids, Magiciens, Knights Templar, Sun Riders en the Lady of the Wolf, desperate try to turn the danger of Wearers of the Black Crown. Grint is a story for the Queeste system and contains 84 cards."

Name: Tin Star
Description: Publisher Description Gunfights and characters in this old West role-playing and miniatures set of rules, with character types, floorplans, two pull-out pages for adentures with pre-rolled characters. BGG Description This is a set of rules for recreating tactical gunfights and shoot-outs in the setting of the American Wild West. The rules cover everything that is needed to play including character design and stats, scenario creation, and rules for campaign games. The gunfights take place as a table top miniatures game using 25mm scale figures. There are floor plans included in the rule book.

Name: Dungeoneer's Action Art 8001
Description: Figure Set for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: "Gary Gygax, one of the "Founding Fathers" of Adventure Gaming, is the guiding hand behind the development of Advanced Dungeons & Dragons into the extremely popular family pastime that it is today. Gary has designed the figures in this set to be the ideal small party to explore any Dungeon. Each figure has been meticulously sculpted to ensure complete fidelity to the Advanced Dungeons & Dragons Cosmology" Figures included: 1. Wizard 2. Lantern Bearer 3. Cleric 4. Paladin 5. Gnome 6. Elf Female/Magicuser 7. Thief 8. Female Fighter 9. Crossbow Man 10. Javelin man 11. Ranger 12. Fighter/Spear & Bow

Name: Spies of Lightelf
Description: From the Forward: "The Spies of Lightelf is the second booklet in our series of works titled "The Wilderness Project". Each booklet covers an area of wilderness in the Lands of the City States in great detail: any villages in the area have various establishments and prominent NPCs listed, history and way of life of the people briefly described, and a list of interesting events and encounters for passing adventurers.... This particular booklet contains maps of twenty-two five-mile hexes in the area of the Wood of the Gnomes, including the Gnomes' village of Lightelf and the village of Palewood, inhabited by Grey Elves.

Name: Encounters in the Phoenix Quadrant
Description: From the Introduction: Encounters in the Phoenix Quadrant is the first in a series of mini-adventures. The encounters can be used as is or added to a campaign as a source for non-player characters. User summary: Consists of descriptions and deckplans for three different starships along with dossiers for the NPC crew members. There are no adventure scenarios, per se.

Name: The Compleat Fantasist - How To Convert The Major Fantasy Role Playing Systems To Each Other
Description: From the back cover: AT LAST!...no longer need characters from one system to be stuck there. And never more will you have to arbitrarily decide how to change personal statistics to fit a different set of rules. Fantasy roleplaying veteran Mark Wagner gives guidelines to help convert many parts of each system the others, and describe aspects that don't concert well.

Name: Woodland Adventurers 5003
Description: Figure Sets for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: Grenadier Models Inc. is proud to present the official Advanced Dungeons & Dragons figure line. The figures in this collection were designed with the cooperation of T.S.R. Hobbies Inc., the creators of Advanced Dungeons & Dragons to ensure complete fidelity to the Advanced Dungeons & Dragons cosmology. This fantasy figure set was specifically created for the fantasy role playing gamer

Name: Fantasy Set 01 - Player Characters
Description: The figures in this set include all the basic character types you'll need for any fantasy game. Use them for your personal figures, or let them represent non-player characters. Each figure has a brief description on its base - but don't let that limit your games! Most of these figures can represent several different kinds of character - see below. Copyright 1980 Steve Jackson Games

Name: Shadows
Description: From inside the binder on the first page: The Traveller Adventure Titled Shadows was used as the Traveller Tournament situation at WinterWar 1980, held at the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign, January 18, 19 and 20, 1980 Pre-Publication Draft This is 3-hole punched in a Black "official" Traveller Binder. These were sold at the GDW booth at Origins 1980 is is believed (I'm trying to follow up with Marc Miller on this). I know that at least 10 were sold and perhaps more. This was eventually turned in one of the two adventures in Traveller: Double Adventure #1

Name: Demon's Pawn
Description: In Demon's Pawn characters of 4th-8th level must face down the demon lord Arekitach in his citadel to save their very souls.

Name: Ravensgate (2nd Edition)
Description: Ravensgate provides an introduction to the slums and back alleys of Carzal, the capital of Ilchania. For character levels 1-4.

Name: Sector 57: Tournament Science Fiction
Description: user summary A tournament SF scenario for 2 players based (roughly) on classic Traveller. From p.20 of the referee's guide: Many of the concepts in Sector 57 have been adopted from Traveller game elements. You will note that H'alster and Monorian are glorified characters from the GDW system. NOTE: Traveller is not needed to play this scenario. The module contains the following components: 24 page 8.5x11" referee's guide 11x25.5" (trifold) referee's shield Players situation sheet (8.5x11") 2x player character info sheets (8.5x11") 3 additional 8.5x11" sheets with figures/maps for the scenario

Name: Supergame (1st Edition)
Description: The first comicbook superhero role playing game to give you: Complete rules and combat systems for any role playing situation Total player control in character construction The only fully quantified, unlimited combination armor system of any fantasy role playing game

Name: Adventuring Party 2013
Description: Figure Set for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: "This 25mm fantasy figure set is specifically created for the fantasy role playing gamer. ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS is a trademark owned and licensed by TSR Hobbies, Inc. The policy of GRENADIER MODELS is to constantly upgrade our product & we reserve the right to alter specifications and contents without prior notice." This set includes: A. Faf- Armored Dwarf w/War Axe B. Rayr- Half-Elf Fighter/Magic User C. Norien- Female Magic User D. Snow-Slinging Druid E. Charn-Warrior in Plate F. Morhir-Monk with Spear G. Kereo-Cleric in Plate w/Flail H. Dent- Bard with Bag-pipes I. Mellison-Halfling Fighter/Thief J. Choff-Female Ranger in Splint

Name: Mountain of Mystery
Description: from back cover: Fantastic wizardry and fierce guardians defend the underground fortress of the Clerics of Athena. A complete adventure, or the beginning of a superb 3-part campaign.

Name: GamesMaster Catalog:  A Comprehensive Illustrated Guide to Games
Description: From the back cover This is the first comprehensive catalog on games. The new fully-illustrated GamesMaster Catalog contains photographs, descriptions and prices of the products manufactured by over 190 games companies. Nearly half of the 1,000 items listed are in full color. The GamesMaster Catalog is the most complete and up-to-date source available - an essential reference book for every game enthusiast! Contents Introduction Fantasy Role-Playing Games, Adventures & Playing Aids Fantasy & Mythology Games Science Fiction & Science Fantasy Games Historical & Contemporary Games Gaming Miniatures & Miniature Rules Accessories for Gaming Computer, Video & Electronic Games Educational & Children's Games Sports Games Leisure Time & Family Games Books & Magazines Play-By-Mail (PBM) Games Future Releases Manufacturers Index Game Category Index Product Index 1981 Game Convention Calendar

Name: Dawnworlds, Gateway to the Open Cluster
Description: From the back of the book: Dawnworlds, The Gateway to the Open Cluster is a unique and exciting new space sector supplement for any S.F. roleplaying game. Dawnworlds is different from other space supplements in that it is complete with history, detailed planet specifications, ship diagrams and deck plans, encounter matrix, and new fully described technologies. The Open Cluster is fully maped(sp) and DAWNWORLD is written like a S.F. novel with lavish NPC developement(sp). Also, it is written by published S.F. writters(sp) for adult S.F. enthusiasts. The writters(sp) have sfollowed the guidelines that DAWNWORLD must be different and excitingm(sp) not just different; strange and interesting, not just weird; planned, logical, and realistic, not just a hodgepodge of ideas pulled from various S.F. books. In this attempt the writters(sp) have suceeded(sp) admirably and have created a fantastic framework for the referee to spin his space adventure on. The book is stapled sheets inside a 4-fold map that make up the cover. Although it claims to be for any system, much of the terminology within is very traveller specific (jump drives, Xboat routes, etc.)

Name: Fangs
Description: A collection of pregenerated stats for Gloranthan creatures included in the second edition box set. It includes everything from major Gloranthan intellegent races to mounts and wild animals. It similar to Foes created by the same author.

Name: Arduin Character Sheets Combined Pak
Description: Includes twenty-four different characters on individual sheets. Intended for use with Arduin but advertised as "compatible with most fantasy role-playing systems". Included various races, classes, and genders. Includes one character sheet (single page, single-sided); remaining pages are full-page black-and-white character art.

Name: Operation Sprechenhaltestelle
Description: Introductory mission for Top Secret. From the Administrator booklet: "This package forms the special instructional module for play of Top Secret, and as such, is specifically designed for beginning players and Administrators. Due to its special design, it has numerous applications and serves a multiplicity of purposes."

Name: Circle of Ice
Description: From the back of the book: In the battle between the Powers of Dark and the Powers of Light, the boreal domain of the Circle of Ice remains aloof. Snatched from your homeland by the Dark Lords, you must spy out the Circle of Ice for them - and return, if you can. Circle of Ice is third in a series of Pocket Adventures designed for solitaire play with the Tunnels and Trolls game system. To enter the Circle of Ice you'll need a few six-sided dice, paper, pencil, and the Tunnels and Trolls rulebook.

Name: Melanda: Land of Mystery (1st edition)
Description: Presumably similar in systems to Melanda: Land of Mystery (2nd edition). It included character sheets and an adventure titled "In Search of Starfax," which the 2nd edition did not. Edit: actually, the character creation system was different. The second edition streamlined it, but the first edition had some interesting ideas which were sadly cut.

Name: Starship Log Entry
Description: From the cover sheet: An organized listing of all expenses and income for each interstellar trip: fuel, cargo, passengers, maintenance, life support, and more. Referees can use these as a lasting record of the comings and goings of their travellers.

Name: 24 Fantasy Adventure Character Sheets
Description: From the description page: These pre-drawn characters may be cut out and colored in for use with your FANTASY ADVENTURE CHARACTER SHEETS.

Name: A Traveller's Guide to the Marischal Subsector
Description: This was a book about an out of the way sector in the Traveller universe

Name: Mission to Zephor
Description: From the Introduction: MISSION to ZEPHOR is second in a series of adventures to alien worlds approved for use with Traveller™. It was designed as an adventure for characters as a part of a larger campaign or as an evening's entertainment. The charts given are guidelines, so the referee should feel free to add to or delete as he sees fit. Above all, remember role-playing is free form, so change what you want, add what you wish or play as is... just have fun! Mission to Zephor uses Basic Traveller with references to Mercenary & High Guard. User summary: The setting is non-canon. Includes a large full color world hex map.

Name: Monstrum I
Description: Form the Introduction: Welcome to the Varanae series of books. We hope the monsters in this edition will increase the interest and excitement of your game. This publication was designed to be used with any fantasy or science fiction game. The description of each creature will help you understand the personality of each one and ways is can be played. However these are only guides and the personality of each creature can be changed by the Referee. User summary: This book contains images and descriptions of 100 different creatures.

Name: Owlhoot
Description: Publisher Description Campaign rules for the OLD WEST, complete with pull-outs for a town's floorplans, stat sheets for its inhabitants, have a cattle drive, a range war, a posse after wanted men--or live a life of crime. User Summary A campaign expansion for the Tin Star RPG of Wild West gunfights. The expansion includes a large scale hex map of the surrounding area and smaller scale maps for use in playing out tactical encounters using 25mm figures. Stats for NPC inhabitants of the town of Owlhoot are included.

Name: Encounters in the Ventura Quadrant
Description: From the Introduction: Encounters in the Ventura Quadrant is the third in a series of starship books approved for use with Traveller™. It consists of four different ships, from mercenary to a shuttle. Each ship is complete with descriptions of crew, compartments, and floor plan. The encounters can be used as is or added to a campaign as a source for non-player characters.

Name: Rahasia
Description: Rahasia is the original scenario that formed the basis of the latter publications: RPGA1: Rahasia B7: Rahasia It was produced by the self-publishing concern of the author prior to its purchase by TSR. From the introduction: A series of 'one-night' scenarios designed to be used in conjunction with existing fantasy role-playing systems.

Name: Dungeons & Dragons Basic Set (Second Edition)
Description: From the back of the box: "Fire and smoke surround you as you swing your sword and land a mighty blow upon the dragon's head. The great creature crashes to the ground with a thud. The treasure lies only a short distance past the dragon's body. Your eyes gleam. Riches beyond your wildest dreams await you. You race forward to claim your prize, heart pounding in anticipation. From nowhere a huge man dressed in black armor steps out in front of you, blocking your way. The treasure is no more than an arm's reach behind him. "None shall pass!" he warns. This is what it is like to participate in a DUNGEONS & DRAGONS game. DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Fantasy Adventure game is probably unlike any other game you've played before. In D&D adventures players take the roles of elves, dwarves, halflings, or humans. They will wander through dark dungeons, meet strange wizards, and battle ferocious dragons. Playing D&D is like writing a novel with each player contributing a part. DUNGEONS & DRAGONS fantasy game is the original "role playing" game and it is now easier than ever to play. The rules are easy to read and understand and have been specially organized for new players. Anyone can join in a D&D adventure. The DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Basic Set has everything you need to begin playing D&D games. In this box are a rulebook, dice, and a special instructional module. In short, everything needed to start playing quickly, including descriptions of over 100 monsters. So enter the Gateway to Adventure - step into the world of DUNGEONS & DRAGONS fantasy role playing game, a world of endless excitement and thrilling fun."

Name: Castle Paths
Description: Fantasy Paths Series no. 2 The second generic set of dungeon tiles by Board-Craft Simulations combined with Chaosium's "Basic Role-Playing" and an adventure utilizing the tiles contained within. "For use with both 15mm & 25mm Fantasy Figures"

Name: Castle Amber (Château d'Amberville)
Description: From the front cover: Trapped in the mysterious Castle Amber, you find yourself cut off from the world you know. The castle is fraught with peril. Members of the strange Amber family, some insane, some merely deadly, lurk around every corner. Somewhere in the castle is the key to your escape, but can you survive long enough to find it?

Name: The Enchanted Wood: DragonQuest Adventure Three
Description: From the cover... A super-adventure with six completely different starting points to enrich variability of play and duration. 21 Maps and Diagrams plus Character Illustrations.

Name: The Mechanoid Invasion
Description: The first book in the Mechanoid Invasion series. From the inside cover: "The Mechanoid Invasion is the first chapter of an epic intergalactic trilogy. Each subsequent game will pick up where the other left off, but, as with the Mechanoid Invasion, will be complete in itself." The first RPG by Kevin Siembieda, the Mechanoid Invasion took place on a colony world under siege by alien invaders. The game included rules for player classes ("Occupational Character Classes"), combat, vehicles, a native alien race (the Rovers), and several types of enemy mechanoids.

Name: Sea of Mystery
Description: From the back cover: "When you were small, your elders warned you that the Sea witches would come and take you away if you were bad. As you grew older, you heard a thousand different voices curse the pirates and slavers of the Sea. Your fellows told scary tales of carnivorous plants, the dreaded Mesgegra vampire-demon, and islands of beautiful amazons waiting for lost males. On the coast below you sprawls one of the port cities on the Sea of Mystery. As you traveled here, you heard of the desert Yajna tribes and their raiding of the Iderian coast; of the Sardur raiders and their attacks upon Merchant ships; but this has not daunted you. You've always felt the lure of the ocean; now you can realize a dream. You shoulder your bag and march towards the port... to tak ea ship on the Sea of Mystery."

Name: Catacombs of the Bear Cult: Level 1
Description: From the Back of the Book: The Great Road, that ancient highway stretching between Khazan and Khosht, has long been the overland trade artery of the Empire of Khazan. Armed men guard every caravan, for the route is not a safe one. Still, the way has become even more perilous since the re-emergence of the Cult of the Great Bear. Sweeping from the forested hills north of Khosht, the Cultists have wiped out whole caravans, leaving only the bloody ground for would-be rescuers to find.

Name: The Imperial Fringe
Description: The Imperial Fringe is the edge of a large and sprawling interstellar empire. The proper name for this region is the Spinward Marches, so named because they are located on the very border of the Imperium, in the spinward direction when considering the rotation of the galaxy. This area of hundreds of star systems, shown on the accompanying map, is to be the setting for this introductory adventure into the universe of Traveller

Name: Glory Hole Dwarven Mine
Description: From the front cover: "Brave the Deception, the Despair, and the Death Within the Glory Hole Mine, and Capture a Fortune in Precious Jewels!"

Name: Thieves' Guild III
Description: Thieves' Guild III, also known as Thieves' Guild 3: The Duke's Dress Ball, is a supplement which adds lock-picking and pick pocketing rules and new adventures such as The Tower of Tsitsiconus and The Duke's Dress Ball.

Name: Grimtooth's Traps
Description: Subtitled: a game-master's aid for all role-playing systems. A compendium of catastrophic traps, sinster snares, engines of evil, and deadly devices with passing comments made on a folio of fearful fates. In all, one hundred and one ways to influence adventurers, delvers, tunnellers, and all player characters... This is the first book in a series of Grimtooth's Traps books.

Name: Action Aboard
Description: Carrying diplomats, rich citizens, and the most important people in the Imperium, the King Richard, a luxury liner of the first class, is bound to be the center and/or target of murder, assassination plots, theft, hijack, terrorism, kidnapping, and more.

Name: Killer
Description: "the game of assassination" Steve Jackson Games interpretation of the public domain game Assassin. From the back: "Killer is the exciting live roleplaying game for intelligent, creative and slightly uncivilized people ... people who want to knock off their friends ... without hurting them." Has gone through at least 4 editions; information listed below is from the Fourth Edition. The original edition was released in 1981.

Name: Scouts & Assassins (Second Edition)
Description: From the Product List on the inside back cover: Includes an expanded Scout Service character generation system; the new character class of Assassin; and full size scout ship deck plans. User Summary: This booklet is an additional volume in Paranoia Press play aids for Game Designers' Workshop's Traveller and has been Approved for Use with Traveller by GDW. The first edition predates the Traveller core Book 6: Scouts by 3 years, the second edition (1981) by two years. It also includes an 11"x17" insert showing the Serpent Class Type "S" Scoutship in 15mm scale, ready for use with miniatures. The book's Introduction is a fairly glowing letter from Marc W. Miller, informing all "Fellow Travellers" of Dan Rapp's great adventures, and how his work is enjoyed by several of the Workshop's design staff. Differences between the print versions: 2nd Edition - First Printing (April 1981) - has the stock # just under the word "ASSASSINS" middle right of the cover - it looks very similar to the 1st Edition however it has an ERRATA taped to second inside cover as well as the copyright page reflects printing accordingly. 2nd Edition - Second Printing (August 1981) - the stock # has been replaced by the bold capitalized "SECOND EDITION" and the number has been moved to just under the logo in the upper left of the cover. In addition the copyright page reflects the printing accurately.

Name: Villains & Vigilantes (Boxed Set)
Description: From the side of the box: "VILLAINS & VIGILANTES is a complete role playing game based on comic book style superheroes. The game includes complete rules for character generation, secret identities, organizations, devices, super villains, and adventure creation."

Name: Star Patrol
Description: Designer Blurb: "Star Patrol" is essentially an updated and expanded version of the 1977 Gamescience release "Space Patrol". Character traits are generated with 3D6. Traits are Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Charisma, Luck and Mentality. The game had other standard RPG features like psionics and savings throws. Instead of fighter, magic user, cleric character classes, Star Patrol has character professions like soldier, engineer, scientist, astronaut, trader, rogue, and spy/diplomat. Characters can be one of 32 alien races. You can also generate your own weird alien race by rolling against random tables, generating a "general type" (mammal, avian, or even a plant or crystalline derivative), a "general shape" (2, 4, 8, or more legs), size, and even your race's home planet's gravity (low, earth-like, or heavy). More than other games in the "Space Patrol" family, "Star Patrol" makes an effort to develop its own setting ("The League of Star Systems") free of the "Star Trek" roots so obvious in previous editions. The game was expanded by one supplement, the "Star Patrol Mission Master Pack". (from Robert Saint John's Groknard website)
