User comment: This boxed set is identical to Adventure Class Ships Volume 1 (Sleeved Set) but is shipped in a box instead of a sleeve and includes a second booklet and a countersheet of figures.

Name: Man, Myth & Magic
Description: "A role playing game of man's greatest adventures." User summary and back of box: Start off as a Roman Gladiator and reincarnate as ... "an Egyptian Sorcerer, a British Druid, even, perhaps an Irish Leprechaun to face the might and the mysteries of the ancient world!" Boxed set contains three books: Book I: The Basic Rules (24 pages) Book II: The Advanced Rules (40 pages) Book III The Adventures Book (52 pages) plus a pad of character sheets and several perforated maps and charts The latter book contained the first four episodes of a continuing adventure that was ultimately fleshed out to nine by additional adventure modules, all of which required the advanced rules.

Name: Mountain of Mirrors
Description: Endless Quest #2 From the back of the book: You are Landon, a brave elf on your first mission into the land of frost giants and ogres. Will you be able to stop the evil that controls the Mountain of Mirrors? The paths you may take through the mountains are many, and the choices are yours. Only you can decide whether the prisoners of the mountain will be freed or be left in chains forever! Will you use your magic sword to battle the monsters that guard the mountain"s secrets? Will you try to rescue the prisoners who must mine the mountain or die? Or will you run down the mist-shrouded staircase into the unknown? No matter which choices you make, adventure and action are yours in ENDLESS QUEST books. You will find yourself returning again and again to experience new paths of excitement. Will YOUR choices bring peace again to the MOUNTAIN OF MIRRORS?

Name: Death Duel with the Destroyers
Description: [From the editorial introduction.] Welcome to our first V&V scenario, eh? What you have here is not only an incredibly good adventure (though it certainly is that) but also a sneak preview of the way V&V is going and a salute to the way it used to be. DEATH DUEL WITH THE DESTROYERS has been designed to be fully playable with either the original V&V rules or with the new improved edition which is soon to be released. This way we can sell you the scenario now before the new rules come out but still give you a taste of 'em so you'll buy those as well, see? Despite this ingenious plot of ours, I promise you won't be wasting your money - - and I strongly suspect that all future V&V releases will be equally worthwhile. ENJOY!!

Name: Angmar: Land of the Witch King
Description: Front cover summary: Angmar contains: 4 major floor plans 8 B & W maps Cultures, herblore & guidelines for running your own adventures A 16" x 20" full-color double-sided detachable mapsheet.

Name: The Snow King's Bride
Description: Solo-adventure for RuneQuest. From the back cover. The Snow King's Bride On to Valhalavahalla! Brunhild is the comely boisterous intended bride of Snorkil Herringsbane, lord of Valhalavahalla. Your mission is to escort her through treacherous mountains, monsters, and marvels, and deliver her safe and sound to the Snow King

Name: Adventure Class Ships Volume 2
Description: From the Introduction (summarized): This set of starship deck plans is composed of ten starships of between 100 and 1000 tons intended for use in adventure situations. The enclosed sheets present complete plans for each ship, drawn in 15 mm scale. A set of 112 die-cut, illustrated cardboard counters is provided representing ships' crews, Imperial and Zhodani naval personnel, passengers, and other interesting character types. Two booklets are provided. The first presents background material and supporting information for use with all the ships in the set. The other contains descriptions of each starship plus a collection of small craft.

Name: Starspawn of Volturnus
Description: From the adventure's back cover: Two weeks to save a world... Since you crash-landed here, Volturnus has been a planet of constant surprise and peril. You defeated the Star Devil's pirates and made contact with for intelligent races native to the planet. Your adventures with them made clear that these aliens would be valuable members of the UPF -- if they manage to survive. The vicious Sathar are on their way to Volturnus with a fleet powerful enough to destroy all life on the planet. the Survival of the is in your hands. You must organize the defenses of Volturnus. You must unite the races of Vulturnus and lead them in a battle against the Sathar. The outcome: victory, or death for an entire world! Starspawn Of Volturnus is the conclusion of the Volturnus series of modules for the Star Frontiers game. It can be also played as a separate adventure. Starspawn Of Volturnus contains maps and referee's notes, including information on new creatures and intelligent races.The module also contains "The Great Game of the Ul-Mor," an encounter that came be used as a re-playable game.

Name: Crisis at Crusader Citadel
Description: An introductory scenario for V&V. (Included in the V&V boxed set)

Name: Monster Cards Set 1
Description: From the front card: ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS MONSTER CARDS Monster Cards combine full-color illustrations with vital information on 20 AD&D monsters, including 3 totally new creatures, on handy 3" x 5" cards. SET 1 Anhkheg (sic) Barbed Devil Centaur Constrictor Snake Displace Beast Frost Giant Ghoul Giant Weasel Grippli Kobold Lizard Man Mihstu Neo-Otyugh Red Dragon Sahuagin Salamander Treant Triceratops Werewolf Zorbo

Name: Lady in Distress
Description: Lady in Distress (mission module TS 003) was written for GenCon XIV. The module can be played as a tournament event or as part of a campaign and instructions for how to do this are provided. The module places the player agents in the role of a strike team sent to neutralize a terrorist group, lead by Manuel de Gama, that has seized the cruise ship M/S Corona. The cruise ship just happens to be transporting microbiologist Dr. Miguel Salcedo and a sample of his new deadly strain of bacteria, so players must be careful not to contaminate the passengers. The module is intended for 5-7 players and includes 6 pre-generated characters originally used for tournament play.

Name: Droids: A Cybernetic Role-Playing Game
Description: Droids is a mini-RPG about the exploits of robots that survived the destruction of their world by humans. Character creation is done by building your droid part by part, and the game includes an introductory scenario in a military base.

Name: Return to Brookmere
Description: An ENDLESS QUEST Adventure #4 From the back of the book: YOU ARE BRION! An elven fighter on a scouting mission for your father, the King. Turn the pages of this ENDLESS QUEST book for many new and exciting adventures. When it comes time for action, YOU decide: Will you fight the monsters that rush you from the dark castle corridors? Will you try to trick the evil Wererat rule who controls your family castle? Or will you run into the unknown down another corridor? The thrills and challenges don't stop with the frist reading. You can ask yourself, "What if I had done it a different way?" and read the book again to find out. In every ENDLESS QUEST book, you pick the path you want and find your own adventure. Only if you succeed can you RETURN TO BROOKMERE The Choices are Yours!

Name: House of Danger
Description: Reader summary In the fifteenth Choose Your Own Adventure book, the reader encounters everything from aliens to Confederate troops in a mysterious old house. R.A. Montgomery throws everything but the proverbial kitchen sink into this one ... or maybe there's one of those somewhere in this house, too.

Name: The Phantastical Phantasmagorical Montie Haul Dungeon
Description: From the back of the book: Welcome to the flakiest dungeon in the world, the Pyramid of Pallapot the Peripatetic. Here the intrepid party of adventurers will find incredibly confusing conundrums, intensely silly situations, indubitably terpsichorean treasures, inappreciably morose monsters, and inadvertently funny fantasy. Any party which manages to emerge from this dungeon with more than a bare fragment of their collective sanity remaining didn't take up the challenge of the adventure in the first place. This is truly one of the worst pieces of nonsense ever propounded as a dungeon adventure, but it is also one of the most amusing tricks that a GM can pull upon his players. Comic relief for all... but don't say we didn't warn you!!!

Name: Ballots & Bullets
Description: From the back cover: "'They're stealin' the ballot box...' And vote theft is only one of the perils stalking the town when the folks of Promise City must choose between BALLOTS... and BULLETS. It's 1882 and Cochise County, Arizona is the wildest part of the wild west. Rustlers are everywhere. Indians and badmen lurk in the hills. Robberies are common. Against this setting, two hostile political factions struggle for control of tiny Promise City. And YOU are there... as a gunman or lawman, a hired hand or campaign worker... OR as a candidate for office. Inside this module, you'll find... Complete material for a 12 week political campaign 213 detailed building descriptions and a map of Promise City 300 individual character descriptions of the town's residents Seven fast-playing scenarios. You'll also discover... How to scare off opposition voters. How to handle loud-mouthed hecklers. How to hold political rallies. How to stuff the ballot box. -- And much, much more --"

Name: Man, Myth & Magic
Description: "A role playing game of man's greatest adventures." User summary and back of box: Start off as a Roman Gladiator and reincarnate as ... "an Egyptian Sorcerer, a British Druid, even, perhaps an Irish Leprechaun to face the might and the mysteries of the ancient world!" Boxed set contains three books: Book I: The Basic Rules (24 pages) Book II: The Advanced Rules (40 pages) Book III The Adventures Book (52 pages) plus a pad of character sheets and several perforated maps and charts The latter book contained the first four episodes of a continuing adventure that was ultimately fleshed out to nine by additional adventure modules, all of which required the advanced rules.

Name: The Curse on Hareth
Description: From introduction The Curse on Hareth is one of a series of interlocking adventure and role-play scenario modules that have been designed and written to provide campaign-oriented materials for the time-pressed gamemaster. This module contains 85 pages of tables, drawings, maps, and text.

Name: Alma Mater: The High School Role-Playing Game
Description: From the back cover: Alma Mater is a role-playing game in which players may choose to be either a Jock, Cheerleader, Tough, Brain, Criminal, Average, or Loser. Their challenge is to successfully live as a teenager through four years of modern day American high school. However, play is not restricted to school. The game rules cover nearly every aspect of teenage life, including sports, social situations, fights, and hot rods. In Alma Mater, you can act out your wildest fantasies and do anything that you would normally consider exceptionally foolish, suicidal, or down-right crazy. This 48-page rule booklet, illustrated by Erol Otus and Owen Oulton, contains a description of the high school, "Central High" including NPC teacher and student descriptions; and a sample scenario, "Starr's Party". Maps for the school and Starr's home are provided along with a 17x22" color poster of the cover painted by Erol Otus. Six-sided and twenty-sided dice are required for play, and must be bought separately. WARNING: This game deals with mature subject matter and is not suitable for children under 14 years of age.

Name: Villains & Vigilantes Rulebook (2nd Edition)
Description: From the front cover: "Superhero Role-Playing Revised Rules by Jeff Dee and Jack Herman" Select info from the back cover: "You can combat the forces of evil! ...find their hidden enclaves with your super-senses... ...and bust your way in with powerful fists!! Use your incredible agility or invulnerability to ward off their attacks as you blast away with energy bolts, or confront them with your martial arts or weapon skills! Drag them squirming and cursing to prison, to be brought to justice! Become a champion of truth and an idol of millions!"

Name: DragonQuest Rulebook (2nd Edition)
Description: From the back cover: The most powerful and comprehensive fantasy role-playing system is now even easier and more fun in the thoroughly revised second edition. The combat system has been streamlined for quicker and more exciting play, and the experience points system has been carefully enhanced to make your characters capable of more dynamic growth. Now presented in this one volume, DragonQuest is truly complete in one book. Although we are continuing a program of fascinating supplemental material, you need nothing more than this book and two decimal dice to play complete and enjoyable games.

Name: Daredevils
Description: Daredevils From the publisher "Daredevils Boxed Set. ROLE PLAYING ACTION AND ADVENTURE IN THE TWO-FISTED THIRTIES Play the role of an action-adventure hero. Smash spies, fight crime, defeat criminal masterminds and more. Enter the world of hard-boiled detectives, cloaked vigilantes and globe-spanning adventure. Become a daredevil." This boxed set contains the core rules 64pg, an adventure, a GM screen and character sheets.

Name: Valley of the Mists
Description: From the back cover "Come visit Hida Province in the land of Nippon. Be wary, though, for all is not well. The ruling clan is not as secure as they would wish. Enemies surround them on all sides and, perhaps, even lurk within their domain. Mystery, magic, and menace await the bold adventurers who shall eventually descend into the Valley of the Mists.

Name: Middle-earth Poster Map
Description: A 3' by 2' map of Middle-earth, three quarters of which is invented by ICE in order to extend the northwestern part described and mapped by J.R.R. Tolkien and his son Christopher into an entire continent.

Name: What is Dungeons and Dragons?
Description: From the back of the book: "Dungeons and Dragons enables you to play out your wildest fantasies, to battle with monsters, giants and fiends, like the Yellow Musk Creeper of the Hound of Ill-Omen, as you search for fame and fortune in the Caves of Chaos or the shrine of the Sahodogen. From students to solicitors, punks to professors, everyone is at it! So take our guide - written by three Etonian schoolboys who are experts in the game - and boldly plunge into the stuff of dreams - and nightmares! Reality will never seem quite the same..." From the introduction: "If you already have a copy of the Basic rulebook, What Is Dungeons and Dragons? will act as a useful companion to it...if you do not possess a copy of the Basic book, this book will serve as a useful introduction to role-playing and especially Dungeons and Dragons". A mass-market paperback for those interested in learning more about D & D and role-playing games from 1982.

Name: Rat on a Stick
Description: From the cover: Explore the depths of this dangerous dungeon or become a wealthy businessman and own your own fast-food franchise. Feed all the horrible monsters lurking within these pages or defeat them for their treasure. The choice is yours!

Name: Behind Enemy Lines
Description: From the back of the Boxed set: "Behind Enemy Lines is a role-playing game which allows you to take on the character of a U.S. infantryman in World War II. Parachute drops, night raids, sniper ambushes, pitched battles, combat patrols, and long-range reconaissance probes are the challenges you face. Your enemy is cunning, well-trained, and well-equipped. Your ingenuity, your courage, and skill are all that stand between you and disaster." Box contains: Book 1 - Character Generation and Basic Rules. 96 pages of information and tables ranging from character generation, tank data and equipment weights to the basic sighting and fire tables. Simple and complex games can be played with selected use of this information. Book 2 - Event Tables - 48 pages filled with event tables for all types of terrain and situations. In all, 38 tables giving detailed Game Master information and general player descriptions are included. Book 3 - Missions - A 56 page booklet containing three full length missions, four incidents, historical biographies, and 192 non-player characters. A 16 page booklet filled with maps for the missions and incidents is also included. 112 illustrated counters depicting American and German soldiers, civilians and special equipment, are provided. The counters are two-sided, showing active and incapacitated states of the various characters. Four cardstock sheets of the charts and tables most used in play. Also included is a sample character sheet. Two six-sided dice.

Name: Arms Law & Claw Law (2nd Edition)
Description: Arms Law & Claw Law, 2nd Edition is the core rules weapons system for Iron Crown's original Rolemaster fantasy role-playing system. This second edition book combines the first edition versions of the two earlier works entitled "Arms Law" and "Claw Law". The Arms Law portion covered weapon attacks while the Claw Law portion covers animal attacks, martial art attacks, and inanimate object attacks such as those from thrown objects or a fall. The book contained detailed attack charts for 30 weapons and 12 natural weapons and charts and statistics for various critical strikes, fumbles, weapon conversions, maneuvers, animal statistics, armor and shield penalties, and similar text information.

Name: Merchant Class Ships
Description: A boxed set containing 15mm deck plans for six different merchant starships: Star Class Merchant Liner Trader Class Provincial Transport Triad Class Merchant Vessel Magnadon Class Provincial Merchant Seeker Class Armored Merchant Iris Class Armored Packet The set also includes two counter sheets of individual crew and passengers and two booklets.

Name: Aslan Mercenary Ships
Description: A boxed set containing 15mm deck plans for two different Aslan mercenary starships: Warlord Class 3000 ton Battle Leader Hero Class 3000 ton Intruder Transport The set includes two booklets with descriptions of Aslan warfare and the deck plans, two adventure scenarios, details of an Aslan mercenary unit, and information on role playing Aslan characters. The set also includes a die-cut counter sheet of starship crew and troops.

Name: The Egyptian Trilogy
Description: Adventure 2, Episode 1, 2 & 3 From the back cover: You stand before an ancient stone monument at Giza. Though dwarfed by the great pyramids, it possesses the answer to a mighty mystery. For hidden inside, in some secret, long forgotten, chamber rests the power to destroy the old gods and to allow one man to rule Egypt. In addition to all three Episodes of Adventure Number 2 this module expands the Man, Myth & Magic system by adding ... 31 New Character Classes, including Physicians and Alchemist! Nearly 70 new Magic Spells, Charms, Amulets and Concoctions! New Poisons! Dozens of new weapons and armor classes! Rules for Charioteers!

Name: Runequest Character Sheets
Description: Taken from the advert in White Dwarf (Issue 37 - Jan 1983) Character & Adventure Sheets for Runequest Games Workshop have revised and amended the character record sheet as published in the Runequest rule book so that players can have a permanent and east-to-read record of their character without damaging their copy of the game. We have also designed a smaller "adventure sheet" which is ideal for use during adventures. The sheet covers spells in mind, equipment, current finances, damage and remaining hits and power points. You get 30 character sheets and 80 adventure sheets for just £1.99.

Name: Orb Quest
Description: From the back of the box. Orbquest is a Microquest adventure suitable for solitaire play, in The Fantasy Trip role-playing game system. It requires the Microgames Melee and Wizard or Advance Melee and Advance Wizard rules to provide the combat and magic rules for play. Components: Rules, map and counters.

Name: Valley of Trees
Description: An early Mayfair Role Aids tournament module that was later revised and rereleased as FEZ I: Wizard's Vale. From the back card: The wizard, Fez, has staked his life that you can out-reason deadly puzzles and outwit (or outrun) exotic monsters. A Quest to truly test all your dungeoning skills.

Name: The Journey
Description: The second book of the Mechanoid Invasion trilogy did not contain complete rules of play, unlike the first and third books. Summarized from the plot description on page 28: The planet Gideon E is now uninhabitable, destroyed by the Mechanoids. Descendants of the survivors are now stowaways on the Mechanoid mother ship, fighting a guerrilla war and hoping to find other survivors in the ship's labyrinthian tunnel system.

Name: Monster Cards Set 4
Description: ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS MONSTER CARDS Monster Cards combine full-color illustrations with vital information on 20 AD&D monsters, including 3 totally new creatures, on handy 3" x 5" cards. SET 4 Axebeak Black Dragon Bombardier Beetle Bugbear Direwolf Gorgon Halfling Hippocampus Hybsil Korred Leucrotta Merman Obliviax Moss Rust Monster Stone Giant Succubus Su-Monster Vampire Weretiger Wind Walker

Name: Rescue On Galatea
Description: From back cover: The mission seemed simple: rescue an abducted merchant prince from the hands of a rival corporation in time to avert an interstellar war. The one month deadline seemed to be more than enough to complete the mission. That is until the adventurers arrive at the planet where the prince is being held. Galatea, a world made up of thousands of little islands and very severe weather made the mission a little more difficult, but then the intelligent natives, the Jessa, ended all hopes of an easy completion of what was once just a simple mission.

Name: Wondrous Weapons
Description: From the cover... 120 Enchanted Weapons Exciting new aid for use in any fantasy campaign. Enchanted weapons of varied types await you adventurers within these pages

Name: The Wine of the Moon
Description: User Summary Supplement for the Ysgarth Rule System that examines several areas of play. It includes new skills, character classes, an experience system, deities, and more. Also includes an adventure.

Name: Opponents Unlimited
Description: "A V&V playing aid" A collection of villains, casual encounters and criminal organizations for the V&V game.

Name: Monster Cards Set 2
Description: From the front card: ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS MONSTER CARDS Monster Cards combine full-color illustrations with vital information on 20 AD&D monsters, including 3 totally new creatures, on handy 3" x 5" cards. SET 2 Galeb Duhr Gelatinous Cube Giant Scorpion Goblin Gold Dragon Hill Dwarf Hill Giant Hippogriff Ixitxachitl Land Urchin Roper Sabertooth Tiger Satyr Spectre Stone Golem Thri-Kreen Troglodyte Type V Demon Tyrannosaurus Rex Umber Hulk

Name: The Spirit Stones
Description: from the back cover: On the hill near the city, you look down upon its maze of streets and crowded bazaars. Somewhere in Sanctuary is the bandit who stole the most sacred relics of your people, the Spirit Stones. The city seems strange after years of traveling the roads and seaways of Ranke. Recovering the lost Spirit Stones from this den of thieves would be a daunting task for a regular city dweller. Slowly you descend the hill discouraged, but determined to regain the only sacred relics your far flung peoples treasure and to return their meaning to the millennial wanderings of the S'Danzo. from the copyright page: Selected materials in this adventure are used under license from Chaosium Inc.

Name: Death to Setanta
Description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 5 User summary and back cover: Fifth part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

Name: Dark Assassin
Description: form the back cover: An ancient feud between two powerful families is the cause of an assassination plot against a young Rankan noble. You have to stop it. The only problem is that the weapon to be used is a magically created monster. Dark Assassin is an adventure in Sanctuary, the city of Thieves' World. This booklet includes a detailed adventure, new characters, maps, locations for Sanctuary, and Gaming Sanctuary by Robert Asprin. from the copyright page: Selected materials in this adventure are used under license from Chaosium Inc.

Name: The Book of Treasure Maps III
Description: A collection of ten small dungeons that can be dropped into any campaign. From the front cover: "This new and exciting adventure book holds ten complete adventures! The Beast of the Blackwater, The Mountain of Fire, The Temple of Allisstanis, and many more. Each include maps, full background information and accompanying rumors."

Name: FTL: 2448 The Game of Role-Playing in Space (1st Ed)
Description: From the introduction: Faster Than Light 2448 is a role playing game of planetary exploration and adventure in the not so far future. You will become a human or alien in a time of exploration and corporate expansion. Fortunes can be made and lost by the seldom considered individual. You will travel to worlds of wealth or poverty, work hells or worlds built on ideals. Aliens and robots will be your friends as you travel the frontiers of settled space. In FTL you will find no Galactic Empires of Star Kings, Dreadnaughts, Battle Cruisers, or planet destroying Galactic Rangers. You will find real people interacting with alien cultures under the watchful eyes of a police agency dedicated to fairness and peace. Welcome to 2448.

Name: The Forbidden Castle
Description: from the back cover The Cave of Time whirls you back to medieval England. You must solve the riddle that leads to the Forbidden Castle, where riches await - and maybe a way home. But the king wants the fortune for himself, and his army is right behind you!

Name: British Commandos - Book 4
Description: "Commandos were an elite branch of the British Armed Forces during World War II. Theirs was the job of infiltrating behind enemy lines for missions such as reconnaissance, sabotage, lead elements of large assaults, and leading partisans. This book gives all the information necessary to make British Commandos part of Behind Enemy Lines. Character generation, special skills, and British equipment are included. A historical commando mission, Operation Frankton, is also included. This mission involved naval sabotage in the occupied French port of Boredeaux and was successfully completed by 12 commandos. It is typical of commando missions and will show the player what was expected of commandos and what they actually accomplished during World War II."

Name: Darkling Ship
Description: From the Introduction: This scenario is the third in a special series of connected adventures set in the Judges Guild Gateway Quadrant of the Traveller™ campaign universe. The Border Prowler series is designed to provide a Judge with a ready-made mini-campaign. Set up in tournament format with handout sheets of information, equipment, and pre-generated characters, this particular scenario can be accomplished with only a portion of the resources available in the crew and equipment of the Hrunta. Other crewmembers and other equipment will be called upon in subsequent scenarios. Each scenario is designed to be run independently. The Mission Briefing Statements on the handouts give sufficient background information for the play of that particular scenario. User summary: The Auxiliary Patrol Cruiser Hrunta is assigned a mission to travel to the Satyressia and Darkling subsectors to investigate activities by unknown parties hinted at by clues found on previous missions. If anything is found a commando team and a team of analysts will investigate.

Name: Mach: The First Colony
Description: Mach's tagline is "A role playing game of soldiers of fortune in a New World." It's a self-contained sci-fi RPG with a unique premise: Aliens called the Abla come to Earth in the late 20th century, inform humanity of an impending catastrophe, and transport the entire human race to the colony world of Mach. The box set contains three black-and-white 32-page booklets. Books I and II (they are otherwise untitled) contain the rules for the game; there's no clear reason as to why some rules are in one book as opposed to the other. The third, III, is an adventure entitled "Frontier Series #1: Guilden's Farm." Mach uses traditional polyhedral dice, mainly percentile, and has a "sandbox" feel to it. The group is encouraged to decide what their version of Mach is like, develop the starting region, etc.

Name: Newgrange Reactivated
Description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 7 User summary and back cover: Seventh part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

Name: Cards of Power Treasure Trove I
Description: back cover Treasure Trove I - Cards of Power is the first in a series of game aids designed for use with any fantasy role play system. The series will provide the time-pressed gamemaster with fully described treasures, great and small. The great treasures, such as CARDS OF POWER, will also provide additional material for gamemasters who run fantasy campaigns, such as rumors, legends, descriptions of the creators of the treasures and former owners, and scenarios. CARDS OF POWER contains: -The Great Deck -The Cards of Minor Power -The Cards of Thoryomachus -The Cards of Philador, Mage most Magnificent -The Cards of O Bengh - Gypsy cards of power -Magical Treasures

Name: Forces of the Imperium
Description: From the introduction: Being details of the organization, equipment, and vehicles of the forces of the Emperor and his Lord Knights. Includes Infantry, Black Guard, Scouts, Law Officers & Mcercenaries. For use with Laserburn skirmish and Imperial COmmander mass-combat rules. Includes new equipment, NPCs, Professional Adventurers, and minor setting elements.

Name: Space Infantry Rule Book
Description: The basic rules necessary for play. The user is required to flesh out his own universe and create all the creatures and worlds the characters will encounter.

Name: Arms Law (2nd Edition)
Description: A 10-page saddle stapled book with 22 card-stock sheets of tables. The second edition of Arms Law added several sections to the first edition rules: Mounted Combat Pole Arms Thrown Weapons and Missiles in Melee Two-Weapon Combinations Bow Preparation Time Changes to First Edition Tables Rules for Animal Attacks (until Claw Law is published) Conversion Notes to Use Arms Law with AD&D

Name: Rolemaster (1st Edition)
Description: Boxed set containing everything needed to play the game: Arms Law (2nd Edition) Claw Law (1st Edition) Spell Law (1st Edition) Character Law (1st Edition) Percentile Dice

Name: Wizards' World
Description: From the back cover: I wizards' World you can - Explore uncharted Wilderness - Do battle with fierce monsters - Accumulate great hordes of gold and silver - Gain amazing magical powers You create the characters and play them the way you like. The game master determines the challenges to be faced and the rewards to be reaped; you and your fellow aventurers decide on the strategy. This manual provides guidelines for play, including - Character races - Character professions - Magical spells - Combat rules - Monsters All that is needed to enjoy many hours of fascinating adventures in the comfort of your own home is in this book, a variety of dice and a vivid imagination.

Name: Thieves' Guild VII
Description: From the backcover: Windchime & Justiciar Two magnificent swords...made by one of the finest bladesmiths in the realm of the Ten Cities... One of these great blades is to be stolen -- can you invade the smithy of the dwarf Vultar and steal the right sword? Which is it - Windchime or Justiciar? You'll be well paid if you succeed -- but very dead if you fail. It's so simple -- the smith will be away on his honeymoon, he's getting married this weekend -- just sneak in, find the right sword, and leave. So simple... You've been assigned by the Guildmaster to recover the Claw of Frithnath, a mighty emblem of arcane power. It's in the possession of one of a number of travelers in a caravan - but who? and how to get it quietly and unnoticed. You've only got ten days to complete the mission and the trail of the caravan is fraught with perilous encounters -- who's got the Claw and what is it? Also, expanded rules for bows, a guide to the fine art of locksmithry (and lockpicking) and special inborn abilities to differentiate your characters -- all brought to you in the newest volume of a great series -- Thieves' Guild 7!!!

Name: Dungeon Floor Plans 2
Description: An extension of the Dungeon Floor Plans role-playing game aid giving additional flooring, special features and accessories for adventures using 25mm figures. Contains 12 coloured sheets of card with a superimposed square grid as follows: Three sheets representing trees and grass Two sheets representing room features Two sheets representing corridor features and artifacts Three sheets representing still and flowing water Two sheets representing flagstone flooring for rooms Each sheet is to be cut up by the owner using a sharp knife. (From the back of the box)

Name: Caves and Caverns
Description: A book to help a DM/campaign judge to quickly generate and populate a fantasy cave/cavern/burrow. It contains tables that can be used to randomly generate attributes such as: - Cave type, terrain, size, number of entrances - Features: underground rivers, lakes, vaults, geothermals - Occupants: number, type, hit dice, abilities and so on The majority of the book is comprised of cave layouts that are mapped using Judges Guild's Campaign Hexagon System. Part of Judges Guild's generic "Universal Fantasy Supplement" line for any fantasy RPG - though clearly targeted at D&D/AD&D.

Name: Inside UFO 54-40
Description: Reader summary The twelfth book in the Choose Your Own Adventure series, Inside UFO 54-40, marks a return to science fiction. The plot begins with a cliché, with the protagonist abducted by aliens from outer space, and develops into a series of interstellar adventures. This book is notable for requiring the reader to break the rules and mechanics set by the series. Whether this was a good idea can be debated.

Name: Corsairs of Tallibar
Description: From the front cover: "The Corsairs of Talibar were one f the most feared bands of pirates that ever sailed the Nydar Sea. Nearly 75 years have passed since any captain has quailed at the sight of that dreaded band at his bow. Were they defeated in some unknown battle or swallowed up by the sea? Find out in this 48 page adventure."

Name: Traveller Set 2 - Imperial Marines
Description: 57 15mm full-color front and back sided folding miniatures plus a number of small counters with full color weapons. From the Instruction sheet: Full-color miniatures for all your games...with no bending, breaking or painting hassle...at a fraction of the cost of lead figures! Each CARDBOARD HEROES figure is shown in both front and back views, and folds to form its own base (or you can use our optional plastic bases). Collect all the sets - and put a whole army in your pocket! They're perfect for travel and quick games, or for a whole campaign. This is Traveller Set 2: Imperial Marines. The set contains 3 identical cards, each with 19 different figures: officers, sentries, troops in undress uniform, troops in camouflage battledress, and troops in Imperial Marine maroon urban battledress, plus extra weapon and equipment counters. Also available now are Player Characters and Zhodani sets for Traveller. Watch for more releases soon, including Vargr and armored vehicles.

Name: Field Guide to Encounters Vol. 2
Description: Front Cover: Two huge volumes containing rules and guidelines for the new role playing system, 600 familiar and not-so-familiar monsters, provisions for godhood, and intelligent character monsters. Good for hours of intriguing fun!

Name: The Tome of Mighty Magic
Description: From the preface: "The TOME OF MIGHTY MAGIC was written and published as a supplement to the growing field of fantasy roleplaying games. Within this book an attempt is made to bring forth a new series of ideas, i.e. spells. Contained within this book are some spells that the great spell casters Aluap, Eridor, Ida and Nimanril used in their campaigns many eons ago. Now they pass them on to you." A book of spells, designed for 1st edition Advanced Dungeons and Dragons (though not explicitly stated as such). Levels of spells are from 1st to 20th level, for all magic-using classes.

Name: Through Dungeons Deep: A Fantasy Gamers' Handbook
Description: from the author I spent a good fraction of my college days playing Dungeons & Dragons when I should have been studying. In the process, I discovered a wealth of ideas and techniques in how to play well and be a good Dungeon Master. I wrote these up in Dragon Magazine and others. One day I discovered that my list of topics looked more like a table of contents than a list of future magazine articles, so I began writing Through Dungeons Deep.

Name: The Confederate Systems Alliance
Description: Star Sector Atlas 11 is a supplement for Space Opera. This 46 page sourcebook on the Confederate Systems Alliance includes sections on the history of the North American Nebula, a starsector map and list of planets, details of 40 planets, histories of secondary colonies, animal encounters, starlines, starline route map, Korellian Police, Underground Alliance, Q-Section and special equipment, CSA military and unit organizations, and walker-warbot armor.

Name: Corsairs of the Turku Waste
Description: This Scenario details exciting adventures involving space pirates and their prey. Three new Corsair-type starships are included, along with 5 scenarios ranging from ship-to-ship combat to a salvage mission. Pirate crews and their leaders are described, as well as merchant crews and their ship.

Name: Demons of Dundurn
Description: From the front cover: "An eerie silence greeted the dawn at Riverside House the morning after the wedding feast of Arnulf and Gunda. As the day wore on concerned villagers appealed to Oskine to gather some of the guests at the Feathered Serpent Inn and investigate. Oskine instructed the group of guests to proceed ahead and said he would join them later. The guests crossed the empty threshold to discover..." Note: The cover states that it is "Part 1 of the Rood of Parth Series" but no further adventures in this series were ever published.

Name: Dwarves
Description: From cover: DWARVES The Stoutest Warriors in the Age of Legend. Herein is recorded their History, Gods, Culture, Artifacts, Wars, Magics, Mines, Traps, and Enemies. Including Guides to the Ancient Cities of Ostohar and Anor, the village of Fin, and the residents therein. Featuring: DWARVEN RITE, a heroic sized adventure for 4-8 Dwarven characters of 5th to 10th level of experience. This adventure is suitable for use with AD&D.

Name: FTL: 2448 Adventure in Deep Space (2nd Ed)
Description: From the back of the Play handbook:Explore the Universe of FTL 2448 2448 You are a citizen of the future, space is your home, your neighbors are aliens, and you work between the stars. Yours is the life of a human or alien living in a future where a society travels Faster Than Light. Explore the Universe of FTL FTL 2448 With Detailed information for easy to use character generation, 200+ skills, 30 alien races, starships, weapons, vehicles, comprehensive medical and damage systems, equipment, planetary generation, and much much more. Space is waiting for you !

Name: Places of Mystery I - Chilling Chambers
Description: from the back cover Adventure, Anyone? ...Because of the dirt, trash, and wood chips that litter the floor, it is difficult to notice the pentacle lightly chiseled into a single, large flagstone. The dried bodies of various small beasts, including a bat, several fish, two snakes, and a rat, hang by pieces of string attached to the beams. Hanging above the table, is a human arm, the dried bones brown and stiff... ...the rolled-up parchment contains two scribbled and scrawled verses that take some work to decipher. They read, "The green dragon has been slain. The crowned king of beasts devours." and, "The birds s are driven from the vile body of the dragon and allowed to fly to Heaven, only to return again:... ...Bleached bones are found within the "stew" formed by the dissolving bodine... What does our party do?

Name: Traitor
Description: from the back cover: Jubal has made it clear. In three days deliver the one among you who betrayed the Hawkmasks or all must die. In a race against time you must find which of you is the traitor and recover a valuable document. Traitor is an adventure in Sanctuary, the city of Thieves' World anthologies edited by Robert Asprin. Traitor includes a detailed adventure, dozens of new characters, maps, and locations for Sanctuary, and Gaming Jubal by Robert Asprin. from the copyright page: Selected materials in this adventure are used under license from Chaosium Inc.

Name: Vault of the Ni'er Queyon
Description: This is an adventure scenario for use with the SPACE OPERA game system. It is an adventure that spans star sectors and several planets in the quest for vast treasures.

Name: Homeworld
Description: Summarized from pages 20-21: After thousands of years of warfare, the Mechanoids have inexplicably...died. Across six galaxies, in the span of 53 seconds, every Mechanoid everywhere has died without warning or explanation. Except for one world: the Mechanoid Homeworld. Is the Mechanoid threat over? Or is there truth to the rumor of a Mechanoid Apocalypse Machine? This is the setting for Homeworld, the third book of The Mechanoid Invasion trilogy. Homeworld contained complete rules of play and added additional player classes, races, more complete psionic rules, and descriptions of the defenses of the Mechanoid Homeworld.

Name: Champions Set 1: Super-Characters
Description: From the game: These are the super-powered heroes (and foes) for your CHAMPIONS adventures. Lightrune, Brute, Flying Cobra, Lorelei, Hellspawn . . . 37 imaginative figures. One of them will be perfect for YOUR character!

Name: Traveller Set 3 - Zhodani
Description: 57 15mm full-color front and back sided folding miniatures plus a number of small counters with full color weapons. From the Instruction sheet: Full-color miniatures for all your games...with no bending, breaking or painting hassle...at a fraction of the cost of lead figures! Each CARDBOARD HEROES figure is shown in both front and back views, and folds to form its own base (or you can use our optional plastic bases). Collect all the sets - and put a whole army in your pocket! They're perfect for travel and quick games, or for a whole campaign. This is Traveller Set 3: Zhodani. The set contains 3 identical cards, each with 19 different figures: officers (nobles and intendants), troops (proles) in ordinary uniforms, troops in camouflage battledress, and battledress troops in standard Zhodani black. Also included: extra weapon/gear counters. Also available now are Player Character and Imperial Marine sets for Traveller. New releases will include Vargr and armored vehicles.

Name: Heroes and Villians
Description: User Summary from book the introduction: A collection of NPCs for SPI's DragonQuest. Some are set in specific areas in the Frontiers of Alusia campaign setting. Maps and descriptions are included for the Fiddle in Hand Inn, belonging to one of the NPCs: Shipwreck Ampfee. There are short-form (half page) character sheets for the NPCs included in the back of the book as well as a page of blank forms.

Name: Thieves' Guild VI
Description: Thieves' Guild VI is a supplement which includes nautical and pirate rules and situations. Note: This was the first numbered Thieves' Guild supplement not to be originally published as a set of loose leaf pages.

Name: Hela's House of Dark Delights
Description: A Tunnels and Trolls Mini-Solo Adventure. From Back of Book: Within Hela's House of Dark Delights you will find any pleasure or pain you desire, you need only ask for it... this was originally printed in Different Worlds (Issue 22 - Jul 1982)

Name: Wizards & Warriors
Description: This is a hardcover book for High Fantasy that has both the modules: In the Service of Saena Sephar and Murder in Irliss in one volume

Name: In Search of Kelandor's Gold
Description: From the front cover: "It is rumored that the vast treasure of Kelandor I still lies scattered about the once mighty castle. The present lord, Kelandor IX has invited any and all to search for the treasure in return for clearing the castle of the many monsters that have taken up residence there since the demise of Kelandor I. This new adventure is for a dungeon party of 5-8 adventurers of levels 6-10"

Name: The Blue Camel
Description: From the back cover: 3 Hours to live... It's broad daylight and somehow you've got to get hold of this stinking blue camel in order to gt the antidote from the murderous little madman... This is just the first of a series of exciting Thieves' World adventures being introduced by FASA. Each adventure will include detailed building diagrams and maps to assist in play. New characters will be introduced and illustrations will add flavor to the adventure. Each adventure will also feature an article by Robert Asprin. These articles will include an in-depth descriptions of characters and new information concerning gaming in the utopian paradise of Sanctuary.

Name: The Guns of Navarone
Description: From the back of the book: The Guns of Navarone is an exciting adventure based on the classic movie of WWII commandoes on an impossible mission. Intended fro use with FASA's WWII role-playing system Behind Enemy Lines, the adventure allows players and a GameMaster to recreate the movie, or explore any number of possible alternatives. Complete character descriptions (including new characters to give a greater number of options fro treachery and intrigue), thorough maps, and new rules, plus complete event tables and situation descriptions hight this Behind Enemy Lines mission.

Name: The Bane of Bryreville
Description: A city and dungeon adventure designed for "any major fantasy RPG" it is very clearly written for Dungeons & Dragons based on the monster stats, spells, and other information included in the module. The player characters must hunt down and eliminate a goblin clan that has been raiding caravans in the area.

Name: Sword's Path — Glory, Book 1: Combat System
Description: From the back cover: The Ultimate Medieval Melee System At last, a realistic hand-to-hand combat system. First in a series of role-playing aids stressing accuracy, the Sword's Path — Glory combat system includes these features: Simultaneous movement and combat, without turns or written orders. The effects of agility, speed, and strength on combat performance. The effects of weapon weights and designs, stabbing, slashing, or impact. The first accurate representation of armor protection, based on actual penetration tests. And, above all, over 67 anatomical hit-location tables which do away with "Hit Points" and other such arbitrary measures of how many times you have to hit the ogre to kill him. Stab him in the heart and he'll die the first time! This system is fast, simple, and yet because of the data provided, a highly realistic simulation of weapons, armor, and physical damage. This realism, combined with the simultaneous movement and combat system, provides a unique and efficient blend of realism and playability. It has been designed for the experienced player, and is recommended for the age group 16 and over. The system provides the conscientious game-master and/or war gamer the opportunity to inject the ultimate realism into any hand-to-hand combat. We invite YOU to walk the Sword's Path and move up to the Leading Edge. From the SPG Overview section: Sword's Path — Glory is a set of rules covering all aspects of role playing in either a historical or fantasy background, with ancient or medieval technology. This is Book I of the series and deals only with hand-to-hand combat.

Name: Beasts, Men & Gods (2nd Edition)
Description: The 2nd edition rulebook for the Beasts, Men & Gods RPG. After the game's 1st edition of 500 copies sold out, Bill Underwood revised the book and printed a 2nd edition of 1000 copies in 1982. Much later, Underwood revised the rules again and reformatted the text for a print-on-demand revised 2nd edition in 2011. From the back of the rulebook: "What is Beasts, Men & Gods? "It is a dynamic system of combat that combines the facets of realism into a plausible and playable form. "It is a unique magic system that allows diverse magicians and priests without hindering play. "It is a solid form of Fantasy Role-Play that engages players' interests from beginning of play to the end of it. "It's what you've been looking for to spice up your campaign."

Name: The Book of Artifacts
Description: User Summary A list of new magic items for use with AD&D 1st edition. Includes a two-page summary of the One Ring and the other rings made by Sauron described for AD&D use.

Name: Endless Plans 8 (Forests)
Description: User Summary Endless Plans 8 is a floor plans set for 15mm or 25mm miniatures for any RPG. The plans are of forests and what can be found in them. Contents 4 Basic forest floors 2 Straight bridleway 2 Bridlleway corners 2 Large clearings 1 Clearing extension & small clearings 1 Clearing extension & special features 2 Short bridleway & medium clearing 1 Lengths of river 1 Bridleway junctions & river crossings Endless Plans 8 is compatible with all the other sets in the series.

Name: Murder in Irliss
Description: From the back of the book: Someone has killed the prince! As the prince's bodyguard, you are told by the King that you must find the murderer--or die yourself. At any time in your search you can be killed--it all depends on your resourcefulness, your skill, and your ability to outwit your pursuers! Murder in Irliss is a solo adventure--the reader is given the plot, the adventure, and a series of scene selections.

Name: Magebird Quest
Description: Summarized from the player briefing: An adventure for three characters. They are tasked by an alchemist with acquiring a live specimen of a rare sea bird: the Azure-tipped Sea Falcon. The party will join a caravan to the city of Carzala and from there travel by sea to the islet of Harch Insel. Once they've captured their prey, they'll need to return it alive to the alchemist.

Name: Library Data (N-Z)
Description: Supplement 11 Library Data (N-Z) continues where Supplement 8 Library Data (A-M) left off. It includes the first large scale map of the Imperium and surrounds and extra articles on the Imperial Nobility, the history of the Spinward Marches and the Solomani Rim.

Name: Miniatures from M. A. R. Barker's World of Tékumel
Description: This guide describes the correct painting colors and styles for use on the setting-specific miniatures produced for Tékumel. Individual figurines are referenced by Group and Figure numbers.

Name: The Mercantile League
Description: A 64 page sourcebook on the Antares Sector including sections on the history, policies, trade and commerce, laws, governments, defense forces, culture, travel, and security services of the Mercantile League, a starsector chart and list of planets, details of 58 planets, details on starlines, fares and cargo rates, and a starline route map.

Name: Gamemaster's Screen for Champions (2nd Edition)
Description: Two 2-panel screens for Champions, second edition.

Name: Ascent to Hell
Description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 9 User summary and back cover: Ninth part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

Name: Baelnok, Holy City of Arberth
Description: In Baelnok, Holy City of Arberth, the characters are thrust into a world of politics and intrigue in a major city. For character levels 3-5.

Name: Wondrous Relics
Description: From the front cover: "This 32-page book contains new and exciting magical items and mystical artifacts for use with any adventure or campaign designed to bring awe to the faces of your player-characters."

Name: In the Service of Saena Sephar
Description: From the back of the book: You have arrived on an island of fantasy--inhabited by gruesome lizard-people. Your mission is to stop an assassination, before it is too late! An evil queen has set a secret bomb in the castle of a good queen, and it is up to you to find the bomb and defuse it!

Name: The Palladium Book of Weapons & Castles
Description: A generic resource about missile weapons, melee weapons, armor, castles and their defenses, and siege equipment. Includes numerous maps of differing castle layouts. Publisher Blurb: What is the Palladium Weapon Series? It is a series of small sourcebooks that presents historically accurate data and information about ancient weapons from around the world. For use with ANY game system. You heard right. The statistical information is designed and set up in such a way that it can be applied to virtually any game system that uses dice. Ideal for fantasy and historical games, as well as, modern games that use ancient melee weapons (knives, swords, axes, maces, ball and chain, picks, spears, pole arms, etc.). The bow and arrow, and crossbow weapons from around the world Castle and design features. 15 different, real world castles. Each illustrated for a castle under siege. Historically accurate. For use with any game system. Some adaptation required.

Name: Monster Cards Set 3
Description: From the front card: ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS MONSTER CARDS Monster Cards combine full-color illustrations with vital information on 20 AD&D monsters, including 3 totally new creatures, on handy 3" x 5" cards. SET 3 Androsphinx Blink Dog Carnivorous Ape Carrion Crawler Efreeti Ettin Giant Slug Gnoll Grey Elf Jaguar Locathah Mummy Nycadaemon Peryton Silver Dragon Sea Wolf Sylph Tunnel Worm Wemic Will O'Wisp

Name: Operation Lucifer
Description: From the back of the module: In an areas of over 20,000 square miles, Recon Team G-5 is racing against time to locate an undetonated Soviet nuclear warhead. The warhead must be located before it goes off; or before it is captured by the lead units of a Krell advance. But Recon Team G-5 doesn't know where it is... This game package contains all of the information, maps and systems necessary for the Project Director to run this scenario. This package also includes information concerning new weapons, the interior of the Commando V-150 APC, the Morrow Project Contact Pack, detailed cache contents and more.

Name: Aboard the Death Ship
Description: From an ad in the September 1982 issue of Variant magazine: A large merchant vessel has been discovered in orbit around a huge gas giant. Attempts to contact the crew receive only solar static. No exterior damage is apparent. Come aboard and solve the secrets of the derelict if you dare.

Name: Fasolt in Peril
Description: Fasolt in Peril is a supplement for Space Opera. This 16 page adventure module centers around events aboard a Fasolt class passenger liner and subsequent exploration of a MekPurr research base on a nearby planet.

Name: Witches Court Marshes
Description: From the front cover: "Danger awaits on All Hallow's Eve. Witches of the world are gathering en masse. The peasants of the area are going to leave, unless you can stop the wicked black mass." From the back cover: "Twenty-six pages of maps detail the area around the Witches Court Marshes and the village of Grita Heath. Complete description of the witch character class. Many witch NPC's are detailed. Three level lair of Mordridda dungeon under the marshes contains fabulous wealth and dangerous adventure."

Name: Lords of Space
Description: User Summary Included in the black 3-ring binder that holds the core rules for Lords of Space are all the rules needed for play. In Lords of Space, each player take on the role of a Planetary Leader (or PL) sometimes cooperating and sometimes at odds with the other players. The GM in Lords of Space, called the Overlord, has essentially godlike powers over the game. He or she acts as an intermediary in almost all player interactions; takes the role of the populace of each civilization; creates media stories (some based on player action and some not), manages large scale space battles (with the help of a small amount of charts and tables); determines the success or failure of missions, research projects, attempts at exploration based on written plans submitted by each player; and even plays the role of each player's trusted team of advisers. More opened-ended than most RPGs of the era, about half the binder is dedicated to helping the Overlap with the various fine points of game play. The game comes with a small set of Appendices that assist the Overlord with adding chance elements into the outcome of events. Also included are the various forms that each player needs to track their military, government spending, missions, etc. This core set includes Scenario One: The Galaxy of Cerberus, which itself comes with a small packet of Overlord Secrets to spice up game play. J. A. Gregory seems to be solely responsible for this product and he even goes so far as to dedicate the game, at least in part, to himself.

Name: Question of Gravity
Description: From the back cover: The Cube stank of evil and the Demon you slew confirmed your fears. Venture into a place where you will never know what will be up next. Assume nothing or face a fiery death in a true task of dungeoning skills and adaptability. This Adventure is for three to eight players of second to fifth levels of experience. Question of Gravity is suitable for use with Dungeons & Dragons(TM), Advanced Dungeons & Dragons(TM), Tunnels and Trolls(TM), and other fantasy role play systems.

Name: Murder in Harmony
Description: Arthur Overton had everything...money...power...prestige...a mansion...a beautiful wife...lovely children...and an ENEMY who wanted him dead. Your job: find out who killed him...

Name: FORCE
Description: A mysterious organization calling itself FORCE holds America for ransom... Can you track them down and save the eastern seaboard from the insidious effects of Virus S-97? An excitement -filled adventure for four or more superhero characters.

Name: Inner City
Description: From inside the book, Inner City is a contemporary role-playing game. That is, the players in the game assume the roles of the characters in Inner City while playing. In order to play this game, one of the players must assume the role of City Master, the referee of the game. The City Master must, with the help of this rule book, pass arbitrary judgement upon the actions of the players in the game and the outcomes of these actions. The other players imagine themselves as, or in other words, assume the roles of, people in a slightly alters modern-day city. Generally speaking, the city of Inner City is what a man from another planet might expect of an average American city after watching several days of prime-time television. Rampant violence, corruption, good cops, bad cops, villainous criminals, and organized crime are the mainstay of Inner City. So!, be your favorite crook, cop, private investigator, or whatever, and above all have fun! Contents: Inner City Rule Book (40 pp.) Game Map Set of Dice Sheets of Play Money

Name: Field Guide to Encounters Vol. 1
Description: Billed as a "new role playing system", it is mostly a collection of over 600 monsters that can be used in any fantasy RPG campaign. From the front cover: "Two huge volumes containing rules and guidelines for the new role playing system, 600 familiar and not-so-familiar monsters, provisions for godhood, and intelligent character monsters. Good for hours of intriguing fun!"

Name: Character Law (1st Edition)
Description: Character Law, 1st Edition is an original Rolemaster core rulebook which is essentially a player's manual without detailed information about magic or weapons while the book Campaign Law is essentially a gamemaster's guide. Topics include character creation, character classes, character races, character skills, and a price chart. Note: This book was later combined with Campaign Law as Character Law & Campaign Law along with some additional material.

Name: Incedus III
Description: Incedus III is a supplement for Space Opera. This 24 page adventure module centers around an espionage mission on the Abrice homeworld, currently in culture shock after contact with human civilization.

Name: The Kingdom of the Sidhe
Description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 6 User summary and back cover: Sixth part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

Name: The Quest for the Nanorien Stones
Description: From the back cover: Venture to the Elemental Planes and battle exotic dangers to save a kingdom.

Name: San Succi
Description: Recon Play Aid 1. Printed in a large, multi-page newsprint format. The play aid consisted of seven 23x30 newspaper style sheets folded into an envelope. Publisher description: "A 16 city block map of the business area of a small city, with 40 floorplans of buildings in a variety of shapes and sizes. The floorplans can be cut out and arranged in different combinations for scenarios using any modern roleplaying system; they are in 1/72nd scale for use with 20 or 25mm metal miniatures or l/72nd and HO scale military miniatures. The set includes descriptions of businesses, a system for generating NPCs, and ROADKILL - a vehicle combat system."

Name: The FCI Consumer Guide
Description: Full title: Freedonian Consumer Institute Product Research Division Consumer Guide Volume 1 From Introduction: ...Report on equipment and accessories. The player's section is designed for easy reference. It gives the item name and technology level where the item first becomes available. Then it lists information about weigh, dimensions, and cost. Under this, is a paragraph explaining use of the item and other useful informaiton. The referee's section gives the names of three manufacturers, how available their product is, and how reliable. It also explains some dangers or drawbacks the item can have. This allows the referee to choose the quality and price of the items made available to the players and possible things which could go wrong.

Name: World of Silverdawn
Description: World of Silverdawn was a campaign setting on a full continent. It included a full color map (22" x 17"), and descriptions of all major locations and points of interest, as well as residents with whom the characters could interact in various ways. It is now out of print and hard to find.

Name: The Mountains of Ikurna
Description: The Mountains of Ikurna draws the characters into some major aspects of life in the wilds of Uttgart. For characters of levels 2-5.

Name: The Glastonbury Labyrinth
Description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 8 User summary and back cover: Eighth part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

Name: Survival at Sea
Description: Reader summary When Dr. Vivaldi shows up, you know that another hazardous adventure awaits. This time, you set off to sea on the track of what may be a surviving dinosaur. Needless to say, there are plenty of dangers and surprises waiting in the deep blue ocean.

Name: M.I.S.S.I.O.N.
Description: "Adventures in Espionage Roleplaying." MISSION: ACQUIRE PLANS From the back of the box: You approach the island swimming silently, carefully avoiding the patrols by diving deeper. When you reach the island, you frantically bury your gear in the sand before you crawl through the thick grass to the fence. Cutting through the fence is easy, but your heart stops when your foot scuffs some dirt onto the last mine in the field. Avoiding the searchlight, you sprint to the building and enter. After neutralizing the alarms, you penetrate the security zone. Locating and photographing the plans is almost too easy ... Oh no ... a guard Contents: 7 - 17.5 by 23 full color maps 4 - 17.5 by 23 interior building floor plans 4 - 8.5 by 11 full color card stock building interior floor modules 1 Book of rules

Name: Bavenlon: Danger in the Dark Forest
Description: From advertising flier: Bavenlon, Danger in the Dark Forest is a description of the forest and political/relidious structure fo the evil "Dark Elf" kingdom. Is contains descriptions of inhabitants, the major temples of evil gods, the major figures in the forest, new monsters, encounter tables, adventure suggestions, and a detailed map of Bavenlon.

Name: Mazes and Monsters
Description: From wikipedia: Mazes and Monsters is a 1981 novel by Rona Jaffe. The novel is a cautionary tale regarding the then-new hobby of fantasy role-playing games. The book was adapted into a made-for-television movie by the same name in 1982 starring Tom Hanks. ... The novel is based in large part on the apocryphal "steam tunnel incidents" of the late 1970s. These urban myths developed during the infancy of role playing games, generally purporting that university students playing a live action version of Dungeons & Dragons or similar game disappeared into the utility tunnels of the school and became lost, and in some cases died of hypothermia or other causes.

Name: Prey of Darkness
Description: From the front cover: "Brigands are terrorizing the Elephand lands. Dare you enter their haunted stronghold to bring them to justice? Or are you bad enough to join them? The decision is yours!"

Name: Starsilver Trek
Description: A DragonQuest adventure designed for six players. Summarized from the player briefing: The players are a party of six dwarves sent to obtain a rare material which, when made into weapons, may give their clan an advantage in an upcoming battle.

Name: Pirates of Hagrost
Description: From the front cover: "This exciting, fun-packed module brings to life part of JG's Campaign Map One. Numerous wilderness, village, and dungeon maps, complete with fascinating descriptions are crammed into this 48-page fantasy adventure!" From the back cover: "Fury mounts within the Village of Ashenshaft as racial tension between Half-Elves and Humans rises to a fever pitch. Beware the ancient sewers beneath the conflict-torn village; the Undead and their master lurk there! What makes the fearsome River Pirates different from their sea brethren? Is it blind hatred for the Invincible Overlord or the all-consuming lust for treasure? All these and many more fascinating endeavors await you in The Pirates of Hagrost!

Name: Lair of Medusa
Description: From the Inside Cover: Canadian adventure written for a universal mechanic described in its introduction although the recommended "levels 4-7" and character/encounter stats indicate strong xD&D compatibility. Adventure is set in a Medusa's lair, formerly a goblin palace but recently raided by an evil magician with a gnollish horde, with an extended backstory and setting detail. (Internal artwork credited to Eric Nin: posting at http://forum.rpg.net/archive/index.php/t-326816.html would appear to indicate an alias for Eric Hotz)

Name: Druids of Doom
Description: Players must teleport into the Druid's Keep and recover 3 artifacts - ideally without harming the druids, but the recovery of the artifacts is of primary importance.

Name: Goldchester: More Adventures in High Fantasy
Description: "Welcome to the world of high adventure, role-playing games! If you loved Adventures in High Fantasy, you are sure to enjoy Goldchester. This book contains five large adventures, for games enthusiasts of all ages! All you need is a group of players, the book, and your imagination - now you are ready for countless hours of gripping action! Goldchester is an ancient medieval city full of magic and mystery. Since its founding, the town has been conquered by an enemy - leaving a third of it razed and the remainder subject to enemy rule. As the game begins, you may explore the magical shops, its armories, its raucous taverns, and winding streets. Other adventures in Goldchester... Castle Knot - A Dark Lord from this castle threatens to defect from the enemy army. But could this be a trick? The players are sent on a deadly mission to find the truth. Quest for the Heart of Set Te Mu - The players travel to the ancient grave sites of seven disciples of Set Te Mu, the ancient god. The players must discover the hidden secret of its heart and release its spirit. The Dehyscik Canyon - An ambushed caravan brings the players into an area full of enemy troops and within the grasp of 'undead' warriors."

Name: Peddler's Ferry
Description: Peddler's Ferry was a village game setting for heroic fantasy role playing games. This adventure module had a map of the town, and details about residents, vendors, and services. It also described adventures which could be found in and around the town, and quests of longer durations that a role play group could undertake for residents of the town.

Name: Rowsion II
Description: from the back of the book When the Libertus lands at Rogari StarPort on Rowsion II on a routine trading call, events soon make it clear that the capital is involved in a profitable smuggling operation. This is further complicated by events on planet. Cataclysmic changes take place on Rowsion II and player characters on the planet's sun face must survive until help arrives. Rowsion II also includes a smaller military adventure as HCE forces seize the opportunity provided by nature to install their own choice as the new head of government on the planet. Rowsion II is a complete adventure for use with the SPACE OPERA game system. Note that this is not a game but a StarMaster's aid for use with SPACE OPERA.

Name: Trading Team
Description: From the front cover: This folio adventure is designed for characters with Navy, Scout Service, Merchant, or other backgrounds in the service of a large merchant company in the Reaver's Deep sector. The characters used in Trading Team are assigned to the Far Trader Scotian Huntress.

Name: Vampires, Spies and Alien Beings
Description: In this book you can choose to follow three different story lines, a spy story, an alien story or a vampire story.

Name: The Catacombs of He Who Never Sleeps
Description: From the introduction: [The] entrance is made of tough granite and was once decorated with elegant carvings and lettering. Now, however, it is in absolute ruin, choked with rubble from the raid. It is evidently impenetrable, and no amount of searching or digging will reveal any way to gain entrance. User summary: This fan-created, hand-written scenario is intended for low-level characters and was written as an expansion to B2: The Keep on the Borderlands. An overland hex map locates several areas from both scenarios. Written abt. 1982, the scenario has enjoyed with circulation since abt. 2015 as both a collection of JPG images and compiled PDFs.

Name: The Time Raider
Description: From the Back of the Book: You find yourself at the control panel of the TIME RAIDER, an amazing time machine. Push a button and you will hurtle forward into the future. Push a different button and you will sail back into the distant past. Which will you choose? Will you choose to visit the future City of Silver where slaves on a spaceship beg you to free them from their evil master? Turn to page 8. Will you travel back to early pioneer days where you must save the life of Daniel Boone-or change history forever? Turn to page 64. In a TWISTAPLOT book, you decide what path to take-and what will happen once you get there. When you finish one adventure, you are ready to set out on another. It's time to start now. You decide where time and the TIME RAIDER will take you. Happy travels!

Name: Famous and Rich
Description: Fourth book in the Which Way series. In this issue you have a magic wish to become rich and famous. This leads you into an adventure, detective work or several other endings.

Name: Which Way Books Boxed Set
Description: A boxed set of the first five Which Way Books, including: Which Way Book 01: The Castle of No Return Which Way Book 02: Vampires, Spies and Alien Beings Which Way Book 03: The Spell of the Black Raven Which Way Book 04: Famous and Rich Which Way Book 05: Lost in a Strange Land

Name: Cosmic Encounters
Description: Cosmic Encounters is the eighth book in the Which Way Series. As a pilot, fly your first space mission to adventure. Fight space pirates, giant man eating plants or be hunted by alien beasts!

Name: The Fair at Tezkorel
Description: The Fair at Tezkorel puts the characters into a surprising situation at a pleasant country fair. Levels 3-7.

Name: Battlemat (Berkeley)
Description: A vinyl gaming mat with a cloth backing designed for use with water-based erasable markers. Rolled up for storage, they lie flat when in use.

Name: Spindelkonungens pyramid
Description: From the back of the book: Ljusskenet från Tomrös fackla kastar fladdrande skuggor längs längs gångens väggar. Ellieneha ser ner på sitt svärd. En ljusblå låga löper utefter eggen. "Var beredda" väser hon. "Onda varelser närmar sig". Ett svagt släpande ljud hörs bortifrån den mörka korridoren. För 400 år sedan dog spindelkonungen Arach under ett magiskt experiment. Hans sörjande livvakt lät uppföra en stor pyramid vid karavanvägen mellan Detwach och det fjärran Faar. Där begravdes han tillsammans med sina skatter. Den allians av nomadstammar som Arach lett, föll snabbt samman under den följande maktkampen mellan hans generaler. Nomaderna återgick till sitt forna levnadssätt i öknen, men skräcken för Arach och legenden om hans återuppståndelse levde sig vidare in i våra dagar. Ända tills nu har fruktan för nomaderna, magiska tabun och värdsliga översåtar avskräckt gravplundrare, men nya tider har kommit. Även till det konservativa Cerand. En gammal man, Assar Besvärjaren, har länge trängtat efter tre magiska statyetter som sägs ingå i spindelkonungens gravskatt. Med hjälp av sitt guld har han nu lyckats övertala en grupp äventyrare att våga sina liv i Arachs pyramid. Du är en i den gruppen. OBS! Detta är inget egentligt spel. Du måste även ha tillgång till DRAKAR&DEMONER för att kunna ha glädje av äventyret.

Name: Creatures of the Dark
Description: Creatures of the Dark is the ninth book in the Which Way series. Get sucked into a void from your ship into a fantasy world filled with fantastic creatures and tough puzzles challenging your mathematical wits.

Name: Pitfall I: The Tomb of Trent
Description: From the introduction: The Tomb of Trent (Pitfall I) is a dungeon designed for tournament play with eight predesignated characters of levels 1-2. We hope you enjoy this module, which first appeared at Gen Con XVI (1983), as much as we have. It is definitely an adventure that seperates the thinking characters from those of the hack-and-slash type.

Name: Your Very Own Robot
Description: Publisher blurb If you build a robot do you think you can control it? Robots can be difficult. You soon find out as your very own robot, built by you, takes charge. Uh oh! Your robot is full of tricks, but he is also fun and, not mean. But if you think you can control him, good luck!

Name: Journey from Rigour
Description: From the introduction: Will the fertile plains teeming with life be turned into an arid desert? Will the hoards from the Red Tower swarm out over the land sowing death and destruction? Will the remote and icy Queen of Pentacles spark green flame at you or will you melt that ice to fire? The answers to these and many more questions are inside Maxi #2 “Journey from Rigor”. Can you Resist?

Name: Action Pack
Description: Taktische Gefechte in Raumschiffen und Gebäuden Der erste Traveller-ActionPack versetzt jeden Spieler in die Lage, taktische Gefechte an Bord von Raumschiffen oder im Inneren von Gebäuden abzuwickeln. Das erweiterte Kampfsystem aus TRAVELLER II - Söldner, Scouts und Handelsprinzen ermöglicht dies in leicht verständlicher, aber sehr realistischer Form.

Name: Dungeon Floor-Plan
Description: Author's blurb: Mr. Steven Johnson is pleased to announce that he is now in a position to offer the first in a series of Dungeon Floor-Plans. The first dungeon comprises approximately 300 rooms and one mile of passages on six levels. This dungeon is suitable for use in any fantasy role-playing game, is capable of expansion and come [sic] complete with notes.

Name: Monster: Aliens and Fantastic Beings
Description: From a publisher product list: "ILLUSTRATED WITH OVER 125 CREATURES for Role Playing Games. COMPLETE ENCOUNTER TABLES, Treasure Types, Random Creatures and Alienoids.
