Additional material also includes Miskatonic class schedules and on-campus clubs. - Pursuit to Kadath - The investigators look into a bank robbery which appears to have been purputrated by one of their fellow classmates. Along the way they travel to New York, Boston, Europe and Turkey. - The All Seeing Eye of Alskali - A bonus scenario which continues the investigator's adventures in Turkey.

Name: Blizzard Pass
Description: From the back cover: A SOLO ADVENTURE FOR THIEVES LEVEL 1-3 or Group Play Level 1-3 The storm sweeps off the mountaintops without warning, as if conjured by some supernatural power. Within minutes, huge drifts of snow have halted the caravan, and the snow falls so hard it is impossible to see more than five feet. You huddle next to the sledge, trying to shelter your numb face from the icy winds. A dozen half-human forms march into view out of the raging blizzard. The little man-things carry wooden spears or long, unsheathed swords in bony, gnarled hands. Their blood-red eyes quickly fall upon your sledge and the ugly creatures turn toward your hiding place. Will you survive to uncover the secret of Blizzard Pass? This module contains all you need to solve the riddle of Blizzard Pass, including 14 maps, an invisible ink marking pen, a complete solo adventure, a pre-rolled character, and an outline for group play! User summary: A solo adventure for thieves level 1-3. This module took advantage of the 'invisible ink' technology that was popular in the early eighties. Playing somewhat as a cross between a Choose Your Own Adventure book and a traditional role playing game, you selected appropriate entries and used the enclosed invisible ink revealing pen to read the descriptions and outcomes. Part 1 of the module introduced how to use it, Part 2 was the solo Adventure and Part 3 recreates the same locale for traditional group play.

Name: The King Arthur Companion
Description: From the back of the book: "Enter the world of Arthur, King of all Britain and master of a thousand knights. Explore the beauty and splendor of the legendary world of Camelot and the Round Table; experience its magic and mystery. Written in a warm and entertaining style, The King Arthur Companion contains almost 700 entries, fully cross-referenced and annotated. It includes a 'who's who' of Arthurian legend; a 'what's what' of famous weapons and artifacts; a 'where's where' of many geographical locations from actual maps of ancient Britain; and a 'when's when' full chronology of Arthur's reign. Useful with the Pendragon game or as a reference."

Name: Witness for the Defense
Description: From the back of the book: "The crew of the Enterprise returns to Janus VI, scene of their adventure with the Horta ('The Devil in the Dark'), and finds tragedy. A young miner stands accused of murder and genocide. Will he be convicted by the swift justice of the final frontier, or can Captain Kirk, Mr. Spock and Dr. McCoy find evidence to clear him? And if they do, where is the real killer, and will he strike again - bringing disaster to the mining colony of Janus VI? This adventure scenario for STAR TREK: The Role Playing Game contains everything a gamemaster needs to recreate the mystery and excitement of murder on Janus VI. This booklet is designed for use in campaigns featuring the U.S.S. Enterprise crew, and includes complete maps and floor plans for the Janus VI pergium processing facility, which can also be used in later adventures."

Name: Wizards
Description: From the back of the book: For sheer power, originality, and cunning, no other group approaches Wizards. Collected here are original articles written by the creators of some of the most famous magicians in history. In addition, four of early literature's most famous spectacular conjurors are presented in detail. Fantasy modules then provide the unique oppurtunity for gamers to join on a perilous quest with their favorite magic user. The adventures in Wizards are suitable for use with all popular roleplaying systems including Advanced Dungeons and Dragons. Lythande of the Thieves' World by Marion Zimmer Bradley Aahz and Skeeve from the Myth series by Robert Lynn Aspirin. S. Carolinus from The Dragon and the George by Gordon R. Dickson Shadowjack from Jack of Shadows by Roger Zelazny Morgan LeFay by Lynn Abbey plus: Merlin of King Arthur's Court Circe, the Sorceress of Odysseus Gilgamesh, the first of the great Sorcerers

Name: The Drenslaar Quest
Description: From the cover: Adventure awaits on the watery world of Yarhfahl, as players join the race to salvage a valuable cargo from the Drenslaar, a sunken star freighter. But will they elude detection by hostile forces long enough to complete their task? The Drenslaar Quest is a complete adventure for Traveller. This scenario makes use of rules and information presented in The Undersea Environment, a Traveller supplement also by Gamelords Ltd., and Traveller Book 4, Mercenary; use of these supplements is recommended, but not required.

Name: Thoth's Dagger
Description: From the first page of the scenario: “This is a multi-session, fully-detachable, 12-page scenario for Call of Cthulhu.” Published in issue 27 (March 1983) of Different Worlds Magazine

Name: Takishido's Debt
Description: From the inside cover: 'A role-playing mini adventure for use with 'Bushido

Name: Secret of the Pyramids
Description: Reader summary It's off to Egypt to solve the Secret of the Pyramids. This marks the first book in the series not written by either Edward Packard or R.A. Montgomery since the enigmatic D. Terman's By Balloon to the Sahara.

Name: Death on the Docks
Description: An Adventure for Character Levels 1-3 The only thing not on strike on the Lakefront Docks is Death... "Comrades, when we take control of the union, I promise you those corrupt capitalist swine will pay in blood for their crimes!" "Gentlemen, trust me... once we control the Lakefront City docks we'll have this whole city by its throat." When these two groups clash, the only result is death... Death on the Docks!

Name: Places of Mystery III - Sylvan Settings
Description: from the back cover Adventure here The Viking Burial Longship ...Eerie, bobbing lights give dim illumination through the fog. All is suddenly still as a crow's warning cry, loud enough to awake the dead, echoes in the darkness. As though in response, aphosphorescent globe moves from behind a tree and casts upon a wall of fog a terrifying shadow - the head of a dragon, raised high, jaws agape, its gaze fixed on the path... The Sacrificial Oaks ...The seven stumps, each as large as the thirteen trees that ring them, have been stripped of bark to reveal the twisted grain and intricate root structure. The tops are a uniform, dark color. They are very smooth, as though red brown oil. ...or some other substance... had anointed them to protect against the assault of the elements... The Carrion Crow ...The trees, covered with deep green fungus, drip wet moss in a 15-foot-long strands. The ground flattens as it gives way to water: a yellow-brown blanket covers the marsh, No sound shatters the solemn... oppressive... silence... ...What does your party do? What does our party do?

Name: The Pride of Ogyane
Description: In The Pride of Ogyane the characters move into higher social circles, meeting some of the legends of Uttgart. For characters of levels 4-8.

Name: Tower of Magicks
Description: Sequel to Beastmaker Mountain From the back cover: Years before, a demon lead horde assaulted the lone Tower. Now you must enter the dread Tower where magic has gone wild to discover the secrets of a desperate Wizard. Can you meet the challenge of a dungeon where not only fantastic creatures and cunning goblins, but the very dungeon itself, may be your opponent? An adventure for those who can meet and conquer not only the unknown but also the unexpected. The second Adventure in the Flight of Orlow the Beastmaker is for four to eight players of third to sixth levels of experience.

Name: Dark Folk
Description: From the back cover: Meet the dark races, the foul races, the evil races; the Orcs, Kobolds, Goblins, Gnolls and Trolls. Learn how they live, hate, and terrorize. Each dark race is described in detail with complete towns, villages, fortresses, dungeons and featuring Orcish Culture a special article written by Robert Lynn Aspirin. Also included are adventures involving each of the dark folk that are suitabe for use with all popular role playing systems including Advanced Dungeons and Dragons. Bring depth to the villains in your world.

Name: Shipwrecker
Description: From the back cover: Shipwreckers and Sailors Tides and Treachery Caverns and Cutthroats but mostly... GOLD! First it drew men over the mountains to the desolate coast. For a handful of nuggets, they crushed goblins. For a bag of coins, men were doomed to a watery grave. And now to find its source, brave men enter the endless caverns. SHIPWRECKER Gold and Glory, this was the promise when you were hired. Now as you gaze into the dank mouth of the tunnel does the gold glitter a little less? Does it matter that beyond this portal lie uncharted caverns, the remnants of the goblin hordes, mysterious monsters, and finally, a pirate stronghold? Shipwrecker is a fantasy roleplaying module for three to six players from fourth to seventh level. Suitable for use with all popular roleplaying systems including Advanced Dungeons and Dragons.

Name: Streets of Gems
Description: Scenario description The setting for this modular town adventure is Sontra, a port village at the head of the tide on the east coast of of Dor Provence. Sontra straddles the falls that serarate the Upper and Lower Flowmarch. The area above the bluff is the old village dominated by its 400-year-old keep; that below the bluff is the new village, encouraged to grow because the land there has been free from baronial taxation for the past 40 years. Several plots are intertwined in the village on Sontra. The most obvious is a press gang that operates from the slum area north of the Kingsway. A part of this scenario is depicted on the cover of this product, and it is the party's first encounter with the village, designed to involve them with the module. By the time the party members have solved this porblem, they have been introduced to a shady character who is trying to take over the barony form his failing and widowed aunt; this involvement introduces them to the village's real threat, the power-hungry cable-pull operator.

Name: The Vanished
Description: From the back of the book: "The crew of the USS Enterprise must find out why the crew of Federation Deepspace Research Facility 39 have suddenly disappeared in mid-conversation - before they too are among The Vanished! Recommended for 6 players using familiar characters from the classic 'Star Trek' TV series. Includes FDR Facility 39 details and deck plans, and Tournament notes."

Name: Heart of the Sunken Lands
Description: From the introduction: The Heart of the Sunken Lands is a detailed wilderness adventure module designed to be used with the Tome of Midkemia. With slight modifications it can be used with most currently available fantasy role playing games. This Booklet Contains: 1 Information about the origins, climate, history, and inhabitants of the Sunken Lands. 2 Encounter charts detailing the creatures, plants, and objects to be found in the Sunken Lands and the relative chances of encountering them. 3 Detailed descriptions and maps of major encounter areas, including a room by room breakdown of the Ishapian temple and a section by section breakdown of the People of the Pit area. 4 Gamemaster's maps of the entire Sunken Lands at the same scale as the Player's map, showing detailed terrain features, major trails and places of interest in the Sunken Lands.

Name: Hearts & Minds
Description: Recon Module 2. Back cover description: "In Hearts & Minds your Recon character is a member of a Special Forces A Team organizing and training a Montagnard village in the Central Highlands of the Republic of Viet Nam." "For daily events in the village there is a 2d100 table of common occurences, ceremonies, and VC planted rumors." "For patrols with your Montagnard Strikers (Strike Force) there is a new set of Contact Tables -- covering terrain, pak time, and extensive VC/NVA contacts along the Ho Chi Minh trail (including a random tunnel system)."

Name: Stormhaven
Description: From the back cover: Thunder shatters the stillness of the night while hulking monsters walk Savage Island . . . well paid mercenaries stalk the renegade Tigers in a race to prevent the theft of confidential industrial secrets . . . a KGB officer is found dead of a strange disease, clutching a note that suggests a unsuspected lurks inside Stormhaven . . . STORMHAVEN is the setting for contemporary role playing game adventures. Set on Savage Island in Lake Champlain, Stormhaven is the fine mansion owned by Kenneth Allard, president of Allard Technologies. Life at Stormhaven is lived in a grand style, and the secret work that goes on in the laboratory beneath the mansion is innovative. Many interesting guests (most of them with more than one reason to visit) can be found in Stormhaven. STORMHAVEN contains two booklets: one of descriptions of buildings, people, procedures and adventures, the other of maps and floor plans. Stats for Mercenaries, Spies and Private Eyes and Espionage! are included. As an added bonus, enclosed are 23 Cardboard Heroes for the main characters and security teams, created exclusively for this adventure!

Name: Lee's Guide to Interstellar Adventure
Description: From the back cover: The journals of the noted galactic wanderer Aramais P. Lee have now been converted into a referee's aid suitable for use in any Traveller campaign. LEE'S GUIDE (Vol. 1) provides full planetary specifications and detailed plot outlines for 10 worlds in which the situations taking place on the planet form the basis for a varied range of adventure opportunities, suitable for both small parties and large groups. Each world is also keyed for use with Game Designers' Workshop's Spinward Marches and Solomani Rim sectors. As an additional bonus, this volume also presents a series of adventures centered around the X-Boat system, the Imperium's primary communications network. Whether you're new to Traveller, or an experienced referee looking for exciting new challenges, this book will provide months of planet-busting adventure.

Name: Thieves' Guild IX
Description: Escape from the Ashwood Mines From the back cover: Breakout! A foreign nobleman, Drak, has been treacherously arrested — and sentenced to a lifetime of hard labor in the backwater silver mines of Ashwood. You and your party of adventurers have been hired to secure his release. Can you succeed, before the grueling environment and the sadistic guards send Drak to an early grave? Or will you, too, wind up digging silver til the end of your days?

Name: Dragon of Doom
Description: Endless Quest 13 Product description: The evil magic-user Zed has used a powerful, forbidden spell to summon forth the mighty Shen, the dreaded black Dragon of Doom! Unless you can do something to stop him, Zed will turn Shen loose on the world to spread death and destruction wherever he goes. The perils you face are many, and the choices are yours! Will you follow Zed to the desolate End of the World and try to prevent his sinister rendezvous with the Dragon of Doom? Or will you instead proceed to the fabled Dragon Castle in an attempt to foil Zed's evil plan? In every ENDLESS QUEST book, action and adventure are yours. You will find yourself returning again and again to experience new paths of excitement. And if your efforts are successful, you will have saved the entire world from the DRAGON OF DOOM.

Name: The Werewolf of Europe
Description: Adventure 3 From the back cover: Northern Europe, 1 A.D. The pale, full moon begins to rise, heralding another night of terror in the small, isolated village of Tharn Grivet. You, along with your hastily assembled comrades, check your seemingly useless weapons before posting guards and going to bed. Laying on your raised pallet, your mind is filled with the horror that grips this place. Somewhere, nearby a monster, virtually immune to weapons and magic alike, is waiting to kill again. Each morning brings the discovery of a new, mangled body, today the victim had been one of your comrades who had been guarding in the predawn hours. Suddenly the night is shattered by a shrill howling. Grimly determined and sickeningly afraid, you leap from your bed, grab your pitiful weapons, and move out into the erie night. As your small group assembles their faces are filled with resolve. There have been too many deaths already, there can be no more. Tonight, for better or for worse, you will find .... the WEREWOLF of Europe

Name: Agents of Rebellion
Description: Agents of Rebellion is a supplement for Space Opera. This 28 page adventure module centers around a team of adventurers caught up in events leading to the outbreak of a rebellion against the Korellian Empire.

Name: Spell of the Winter Wizard
Description: From the cover: Only you can save the kingdom - Warzan, the evil winter wizard, has cast a spell of ice and snow over the land of Eternal Spring. You must rescue the captive Wizard Alcazar and stop Warzen...if you can! His army of orcs and boars is mighty and his magic is powerful! What will you do? The paths you may take are many, and the choices are yours! Will you try to persuade the powerful Frost Giant to aid you in the battle against the winter wizard? Will you sail to the fair Island of Etaknon and attempt to enlist the aid of the mighty ThorTak? Or will you seek out the magical Crimson Flame Mushroom, cure the ailing Alcazar, and use his powerful magic against Warzen?

Name: Ship Construction Manual
Description: This manual is themed with "Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan" on the cover. From the back cover: "Build any starship for use with Star Trek: The Role Playing Game with this easy to use rules supplement. Any ship from the mighty Enterprise to an Orion Blockade Runner can be constructed and the player reference panels drawn. Basic data is included for the Federation, the Klingon, Romulans, Gorn, and Tholians. Now players can design new Federation ship classes and gamemasters new adversaries for Star Trek: The Role Playing Game."

Name: Prisoner of the Ant People
Description: Reader summary With a title right out of a '50s monster movie, Prisoner of the Ant People, the 25th entry in the Choose Your Own Adventure series, provides just about everything that title promises... This is R.A. Montgomery's tenth book in the series.

Name: Keep of the Ancient King
Description: From the Face-page: You are Amy. Your horse, Acorn, has been stolen by the evil Shadow Rider. When the Good Knight of the Golden Dragon and his warriors pass your father's inn on their way to the KEEP OF THE ANCIENT KING, you volunteer to lead them. Maybe you'll find your horse there. From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

Name: Plague of Terror
Description: from Module Scenario Description The setting of play in this module is Wentworth, a small manor village of historic and strategic importance. Wentworth occupies the flat land to the west of a narrow pass, Shaltor Notch, in one of the branches of the Snowpeak Mountains. Through this notch runs the Kingsway, a paved road many centuries old, patrolled and maintained by the king. Spanning the notch is a fortification that has been turned into an inn. He who controls the Kingsway controls the flow of goods and troops across the province. He who controls Shaltor Notch controls the Kingsway, and he who controls Wentworth Manor controls Shaltor Notch. The basic plot deals with the takeover of Wentworth Manor, with the intent to capture Shaltor Notch, disrupt trade, and break the King's hold on this part of the province. When the module begins the baron's wife has died mysteriously, the baron is paralyzed with grief, the baron's son is a puppet of the man taking over, who is scheduled to marry the baron's daughter.

Name: The Harrensa Project / The Stazhlekh Report
Description: Double adventure, literally two back-to-back adventures from FASA with no indication of which is the first. The Harrensa Project: (From Introduction) This adventure, The Harrensa Project deals with industrial espionage, personal combat, and survival in various environments. High Guard, Citizens of the Imperium and Ordeal by Eshaar may prove useful. Players may wish to refer to issues of High Passage magazine for additional data on The Old Expanses. The Stazhlekh Report: (From Introduction) This advenure, The Stazhlekh Report, centers on a commando operation geared to gain vital military intelligence that may influence the course of an interstellar conflict. FASA's Trail of the Sky Raisers presents further background on Desaekhe and the Descaronthe Hegemony and the League of Suns which preceded the war that figures in their adventure.

Name: The Horror of High Ridge
Description: Reader summary In the 27th book in the Choose Your Own Adventure series, the reader is treated to a good old-fashioned ghost story. Spooks and wraiths abound as a peaceful trip to the mountains takes on a decidedly sinister and deadly turn. This is Julius Goodman's second book in the series.

Name: Ship Recognition Manual: The Klingon Empire (1st Edition)
Description: From the back of the book: "Ship Recognition Manual: The Klingon Empire contains 14 vessels of the Klingon Imperial Navy. All available data on each ship is presented in this volume, including: Tonnage, Crew, Range, Warp Speeds, Weaponry, and all game information. This first volume contains the following ships: K-23 ESCORT, W-2 WARP SHUTTLE, L-6 GUNBOAT, K-5 PATROL CRAFT, S-8 MOBILE DRY DOCK, L13 BATTLESHIP, D-7 LT. CRUISER, K-26 ESCORT, S-4 FLEET SERVICE SHIP, L-9 FRIGATE, D-10 HEAVY CRUISER, D-11 DESTROYER, D-18 DESTROYER, and the K-3 GUNBOAT."

Name: Robbers and Robots
Description: An Endless Quest #9 Product Description: The reader, as Terry Morton, becomes involved in an attempt to steal the plans for a top secret security robot from his uncle's electronics factory. --This text refers to an out of print or unavailable edition of this title.

Name: The Menace Beneath the Sea
Description: From publisher blurb: THE SECRET OF TAHUKA HIVA It should have been a simple job, really more of a lark than work, but the drone of an engine where none should be was the herald of danger. It was just the beginning of a hazardous flight for life. SPECIAL FEATURE: ADDING NINJA TO THE DAREDEVILS WORLD Deadly Japanese assassins can now be a part of your daredevil adventures. This section includes background and details concerning these black-clad figures of the night. As a special bonus, developed Ninja characters are provided to include in the adventures in this issue and/or to stimulate the creation of other adventures by creative gamemasters. THE MENACE BENEATH THE SEA The evil Doctor Ling returns as he attempts to further his plot involving the Nine Stones of Uighur. Can the daredevils breach his stronghold? Can they defeat his minions? Can they do it in time to save the world from Ling's insidious plan? Find out in this sequel to 'Deadly Coins.'

Name: The Ruins of Chicago
Description: From the back of the module: Recon Team G-12C must enter the nightmare world of the ruins of Chicago in order to locate the remains of a once proud university. Within the wreckage of the university there may lie the accumulated knowledge of our world. But also within Chicago are cannibalistic clans; a City Machine; and the enigmatic Fort Morrow. And finally, Recon Team G-12C must stop a war. This game package contains all of the information, maps and systems necessary for the Project Director to run this scenario. This package also includes information concerning new weapons, the interior of the Commando Ranger, city encounters for Chicago, and the deadly inhabitants of 22nd century Chicago.

Name: The Forgotten King
Description: From the back cover: The High Council of Pellham is floundering. A restless populace claims nothing has gone right since the last king was deposed, nearly two centuries ago, and the political situation is turning ugly. Hope comes from a monastic order, the Brothers of Brie, in the form of an ancient prophecy. A long-forgotten king will rise in the hour of Pellham's greatest need and restore order, according to the Brothers. Can you prove that this is indeed the time for prophecy? Can you recover the keys to the tomb of the Forgotten King? The prophecy will not come to pass unless YOU succeed! The Forgotten King is the first in a series of four RPGA™ modules concerning the Prophecy of Brie. It is an AD&D™ adventure for 6–10 characters, levels 4–7, that was originally run as a tournament at the GEN CON® XVI Convention. It contains two wilderness adventures, background information, maps and detailed encounter keys.

Name: Fantasy Gamer's Compendium
Description: From the front cover: "Contains the complete Books of Mystery, Monsters, Demons, Sorcery, Shamans and Treasures. This fantasy role-playing supplement works with all systems." Revised & Expanded edition includes "Treasures I/II" User Summary: The Books of Mystery sets up a new character class, the "Lords of Mystery." Monsters and Demons are what you would think, Sorcery and Shamans are spells and class differences from standard Magic Users. Treasure contains items from Earth lore and mythology; Treasures II has items from the science fiction future.

Name: Hârn
Description: This is the original presentation of the Hârn setting which contained a map; Hârnview, a 32 page overview of Hârn; and Hârndex, a 64 page index containing entries on the history, geography, religions, cultures, politics, and economics of Hârn. The 3 items were packaged in a folder.

Name: Lair of the Freebooters
Description: A campaign setting for the world of Haven. It provides details on the Southron Realms / Biracean Sea area of Haven - 3,000 miles south and east of the Free City of Haven. The northern part of this area is the continent of Erinoir and the Eastern Realms of the once-great Namori Empire. The rest of the area is comprised of independent island nations populated mainly with pirates. Several towns are detailed, along with adventure possibilities.

Name: Tarantis
Description: The last original Judges Guild product. This is another detailed City State with surrounding environment. Includes information on the history, government, customs, religions, and military of Tarantis in addition to all the shops and other buildings. Includes: Two 96-page books A 22" x 34" map of the entire Wilderlands A 20" x 28" map of the City State of Tarantis 2 maps (Judge & Player) of Campaign Map #4/5-Tarantis/Valon

Name: The Vanishing Investigator
Description: For Character Levels 4-7 From the Back Cover: January 1927 - Lakefront City For too long the city has suffered in the hands of crime and corruption: by fraud and forgery, by bootlegging and bribery, the syndicate has tightened its iron grip on the lives of decent citizens. But now the Senate Subcommittee is in town and a showdown is in the works. On one hand...Senator Andrew Barrington, whose reputation as a watchdog for decency and justice has preceded him to these hearings. On the other...Stephano Luchinetti, whose charm, wit, and sophistication hide the cold nerve of a kingpin of crime. The law may be on Barrington's side, but Luchinetti holds a trump card he plans to play in a cruel hand—one that you and your players will discover when you play The Vanishing Investigator!

Name: Trader Captains and Merchant Princes
Description: From inside the front cover: "Trade, speculation, and high finance in the Star Trek universe. A supplement for Star Trek: The Role-Playing Game." Back cover has a Star Map with the Original Federation Boundaries, Tholian Space, Klingon Empire, Gorn Space and Romulan Empire plus Neutral Zone.

Name: Hero of Washington Square
Description: Endless Quest #7 From back of book: You are pursued by a gang of cutthroat spies who will stop at nothing to gain the secret of nuclear weaponry. The mysterious green diamonds you have been entrusted with seem to hold the missing key they are after. What will you do? The perils you face are many and the choices are yours! Will you attempt to rescue Sam Brock, who has been kidnapped by the spiess to discover his secret source of uranium? Or will you enlist the aid of your friends Pigeon Mary and Bagel Ben to thwart the gang's plans? Action and adventure are yours in every ENDLESS QUEST book. You will find yourself returning again and again to experience new paths of excitement. And if you succeed, you will have earned the title HERO OF WASHINGTON SQUARE.

Name: Border Crossing
Description: From the Introduction: Border Crossing is a daring mission across the Iron Curtain when conventional methods have failed to gain vital knowledge. This adventure postulates the operation of a Covert Action Team in modern East Germany. Missions like this are he last resort of the intelligence community when remote means fail and there is no agent in place, or (in this case) the agent in place has been neutralized by enemy action.

Name: The Serpent Islands
Description: From the back cover: Your party is among a group of adventurers who have been hired to serve as elite mercenaries in the dwarven wars against the servants of Kaishnalai, at an extravagant salary commensurate with your level of experience. To reach the battle zone, a two week sea voyage is necessary. After one week at sea, the ship is caught in a terrible storm which lasts three days. Late one night, when the storm finally relents, the ship finds itself in unfamiliar waters with the rudder and mainmast destroyed. After a few hours adrift, during which the crew tries various schemes to build a makeshift rudder and find out where you are, calm seas suddenly explode under the fury of a huge serpent; a toothed snake with six tentacles like those of a squid. It attacks with complete surprise and totally destroys the ship before any effective defense can be given. In the heat of the attack, the captain's voice is heard: "Abandon ship!" and "Every man for himself!" Can you face the horrors that thrive on the Serpent Islands? Will destiny lead you to riches and a safe return or leave your bones silently smoldering beneath the mud? Your fate awaits you...

Name: Ruins of Rangar
Description: From the Face-page: You are Erik. On a quest with your brother, Sir Gregor, Good Knight of the Golden Dragon, you travel to the RUINS OF RANGAR, seeking to destroy the evil skeleton king, Nightshade. From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

Name: Agent Dossiers
Description: Official player character record sheets. From the back cover: "Attached is a complete set of Top Secret Agent Dossiers, suitable for use in any Top Secret Game campaign. These dossiers include spaces for all personal traits, vital statistics, weapons, equipment, areas of knowledge, and languages. Space has also been provided for your personal notes on friends, contacts, and enemies. Be sure not to confuse the three. The dossiers are designed so that you may reference them quickly (tie is, of course, always of the essence), and they have been neatly categorized and labeled (as we all know, ladies and gentlemen, neatness counts)."

Name: Elves
Description: From cover: Chosen by the Goddess Em'Rel, your band quests throughout an Elven sub-continent to... RECOVER ANCIENT ARTIFACTS The Dark Elves, your evil adversaries, are aware of your mission and will try to thwart your every move. With courage and resourcefulness, the party has to overcome many obstacles and monsters to achieve your goal. ONLY YOU CAN RESCUE the heirlooms and bear them to Avalin - the the High Elves, who will... SLAY THE DARK GOD. ELVES is a reference source book on six elven races describing their cultures, magic items, and history. The six inter-linked adventures are for 4 to 8 characters of Skill Levels 4 to 7.

Name: Jungle of Lost Souls
Description: From the back cover: Almost a century ago. Durenian colonists landed on the norther coast. There, they raised bustling cities under the cooling breezes from the sea, but, except for soldiers, traders, and ambitious adventurers, no one ventures far into the fever-ridden, mosquito-cursed interior. Even these are usually content to stay close to the outposts, taking an occasional profit when some of the friendlier Taweii come in to sell their pelts, their medicinal herbs, and some exotic, jungle products. You turn and stroll back to the fort. The musical call of the auctioneer catches your attention. He is selling a catch of slaves that a party of adventurers just brought back from the wilderness. Yet, they came back disappointed; like you, they had heard the rumors of great wealth waiting to be found in the jungle, ruined cities rich in grave goods, undiscovered civilizations, fountains of youth, and the bountiful graveyards of the giant, ivory bearing beasts. You have already decided to try your luck in the forbidding jungle. To begin your adventure, go to... Designed for characters of a maximum of 40 combat adds and 4th level spells.

Name: I Signori del Caos
Description: I Signori del Caos ("The Lords of Chaos" in english) is a fantasy role-playing game published by Black Out Publishing in 1983 and created by Auro Miselli and Franco Tralli, with the later collaboration of Fabio Cavalieri, Roberto Della Casa, Giovanni Maselli, Moreno Muzzarelli and Marco Saltarin. The game system has very strong similarities with that of the original Dungeons & Dragons and Advanced Dungeons & Dragons which occurs in many respects as a rewrite Italian. Source: Wikipedia I Signori del Caos available under the CC-BY-SA License.

Name: The Contract
Description: From the back cover: The wizard Fez has faith in the abilities of adventurers. But Mephistopheles isn't as confident. So a pact was made between the wizard and the demon. As each bargained madly to outdo the other's bet, Fez was trapped in a magical sleep. More than just a wizard's pact is at stake as you strive to accomplish the seven "impossible" tasks. FEZ II is a module which will challenge your ability to overcome dangers, solve problems, and adjust quickly to the unexpected. It was the wizard Fez that made the deal with the demon, but it is you who must make good on... FEZII: THE CONTRACT

Name: Evil Ruins
Description: From the back cover: The castle was old when it harbored Celtish Kings. To the Saxons it is only a few walls in the forest. But both knew terror stalked among its... EVIL RUINS This is an adventure for those willing to face the challenge of surviving deadly foes and solving a centuries old mystery. The information you gain may be more valuable than the treasure you gather, for it may let you live to enjoy the treasure. Evil Ruins is a fantasy roleplaying module for three to six players from one to fourth level. Suitable for use with all popular roleplaying systems including Advanced Dungeons and Dragons. comment Ruinen des Schreckens is a German language Generic / Universal publication of this adventure

Name: Talisman of Valdegarde
Description: From the back of the book: You are Gwynne, and you are training to be initiated into the ancient arts of a Druid priestess. But you must test your skills sooner than you expected when the monstrous forces of the evil Lytir threaten your peaceful kingdom. He is Neil, a handsome, enigmatic bard whose lilting melodies on the lute have the power to enchant. So, too, do his emerald eyes, as you learn as you begin to question whose cause he actually serves. Will your fantasy adventure lead you to your first romance, or will the decisions you make carry you into realms in which there is no future? In a HEARTQUEST book, you, the reader, are challenged to PICK A PATH TO ROMANCE AND ADVENTURE.

Name: The Palladium Book of Weapons and Assassins
Description: Publisher's description: "The Palladium Book of Weapons and Assassins is the third book in the renowned Palladium weapons series. Weapons and Assassins explores the assassin societies throughout history (such as the Ninja and Thuga), examining their methods, weapons, tools and poisons. Why did these societies come to exist? How deadly were they? What is myth and reality? What secrets made them the powers that they were? The answers are unlocked in the pages of Weapons and Assassins. Over a hundred illustrations depicting weapons, tools, armour, special constructions, costumes, and more fill this 50-page playing aid."

Name: Star Trek Gamemaster's Kit
Description: From the back of the book: "The STAR TREK GAMEMASTER'S KIT includes the following: 3-panel Gamemaster's Screen displaying all important tables and charts; 16-page booklet of all the tables and charts needed by players and gamemasters, including character sheets for Star Fleet personnel, Klingons, merchant characters, and much more."

Name: Worlds of Adventure
Description: Worlds of Adventure was an early supplement for To Challenge Tomorrow and presented generic "science fiction" setting material for the game master. Although it was published during the 1st Edition period of the game it remained a useful resource throughout the next several editions.

Name: Raid on Nightmare Castle
Description: Endless Quest #14 From the back of the book: You are Kyol, a young Cleric about to set off on an important mission to dreaded Nightmare Castle. Will you be able to defeat the evil warlord Rorlis and rescue his captive, the brave elven rule Estragon? The paths you may take are many, and the choices are yours! Will you enlist the aid of the great magician Canos, even though his powers have been diminished through injury? Or will you take the magical Jewel of Kaibak he offers you, knowing that you may not have the courage to use its mind-destroying power against the monsters that guard the castle?

Name: Blasted Land
Description: A tournament AD&D dungeon from Dallcon '83. The party must find and "neutralize" a powerful artifact in a limited amount of time. The module contains all of the original tournament rules, characters, and point scoring system. Sold as a limited, numbered edition of 1000 copies.

Name: Featuring Deadly Coins
Description: From publisher blurb: THE POWERS OF DR. REMOUX A death on campus causes concern among the students and faculty alike. A dark shadow stalks the collegiate nights and the police seem baffled. Is there a connection? The daredevils find action and mystery behind ivy covered walls. BRING IT BACK ALIVE! A contract from a mysterious millionaire is a ticket for adventure in far places. Double-dealing, danger and death may await the daredevils as they face the unknown and attempt to capture it alive! DEADLY COINS Death for a few dollars more? Hardly. Sudden violence dogs the path of some curious coins in the seething streets of Hong Kong. Just knowing about them can mean death! The daredevils uncover a trail of tenuous clues which will lead them into a confrontation with the evil Doctor Ling.

Name: City of the Sacred Flame
Description: "A complete campaign module set in the world of Haven" From the back cover: Primitive Passions ... City of the Sacred Flame is a complete campaign module, detailing the inhabitants, shops, and other places of interest in Zal' Akhen. This module can be used independently or in conjunction with other Gamelords campaign packs — particularly Within the Tyrant's Demesne, which describes the fortress city of dhar Ankhes on the southern end of the High Valley, where Zat'ak met his fiery end. This module utilizes the Thieves' Guild rules system, but is compatible with any major fantasy role-playing system. City of the Sacred Flame contains a detailed city map, dozens of shop and NPC descriptions, city encounter tables, and two complete introductory scenarios — enough material for months of adventuring. If you like rough-and-tumble excitement with an exotic flavor, then the city of Zal' Akhen is the place for you!

Name: Kobold Hall
Description: Tournament AD&D adventure run at Origins '83 in Detroit. The module title is play on words for the convention venue (Cobo Hall). In part one of the adventure the players must find and gather all 14 magical fire opals to power a pyrohydra statue to defend their homes from the onslaught of the evil Statue of Sliff sent by a neighboring island. In part two the party must place the 14 fire opals in new remote hiding spots. Sold as a limited edition of 750 numbered copies.

Name: Kabal Magic Spells
Description: User Summary A collection of spells for the game Knights and Berserskers and Legerdemain.

Name: To Challenge Tomorrow (1st Edition)
Description: To Challenge Tomorrow: Role Playing Past, Present, and Future Science-Fiction and Historical Rules User summary: To Challenge Tomorrow 1st Edition presented a surprisingly complete and modern role-playing game with a focus on detail and realism. The system inherited many aspects of the Ysgarth fantasy system, including a classless Skill system.

Name: The Demon Pits of Caeldo
Description: From the inside cover: CASTLE CAELDO IS HAUNTED!! The once proud castle of the Barons of Caeldo is a tumbling ruin, populated by rats and hawks. 'Tis said that demons inhabit the cold corridors of the castle and the pits below, and that to spend a night within its walls is to risk horrible death (but that's just a silly old wives' tale!). The King in Tualyn is offering 10,000 pieces of gold to anyone who can spend a week in the old ruin -- and a title and the Barony! Think of it! a fortune for sleeping in an old castle and fighting off a few mice and birds! And the rumors of treasure within its walls -- magical artifacts, huge piles of gold and jewelry, fabulous rugs and tapestries, all sorts of treasure! Just spend a week cleaning up the castle, dusting off the cobwebs, stomping an occasional rat, and the Barony, the castle, and all its treasures are yours!

Name: Ship Recognition Manual: The Federation (1st Edition)
Description: From the back of the book: "Ship Recognition Manual: The Federation contains 14 vessels of the United Federation of Planets. All currently available unclassified date for each ship is presented in this volume of the Ship Recognition Series along with all information necessary to add these vessels to your game. The study of the Ship Recognition Series is required at Starfleet Academy and has become a useful tool of all Starship commanders. This supplement includes ships such as the New Enterprise, Reliant, Larson and many more."

Name: Burned Bush Wells
Description: From the back cover: "No two ways about it: the town of Burned Bush Wells sits snugly in the back pocket of Mr. Lyle Underhay, businessman. Now, unlike some of the other merchants in town, Underhay's dealings have been pretty underhanded. Now your band of riders stands at the edge of town, a cold norther at your back that cuts through even the wolf pelts slung across your saddles. The skins will draw a decent price in the general store, and you'll draw attention: from the good citizens; from a group of tough-looking rounders that seem to walk into trouble before it starts; from a small spunky band of shop owners; perhaps from Underhay himself. Burned Bush Wells is a Boot Hill Wild West Module that provides both town and wilderness adventures set in the dead of winter."

Name: Lost on the Amazon
Description: Reader summary After searching for a lost tribe in the series' previous volume, this time it's you yourself who becomes lost while traveling the Amazon River. This is the 24th book in the Choose Your Own Adventure series written by R.A. Montgomery. In 2005, this was reprinted and renumbered as book 9 in the series.

Name: Circus of Fear
Description: Endless Quest #10 From back cover: You know too much! You learn that a cruel circus owner plans to take over the country. Somehow you must get away and expose his scheme. What will YOU do? The dangers you face are many and the choices are yours! Will you tell the animal trainer, even though he can turn his menagerie of ferocious beasts loose on you? Will you tell your story to the boastful trapeze artist who holds your life in his hands high above the center ring? Or will you seek help from the sideshow "freaks" who want to turn you into a living mummy? Action and adventure are yours in every ENDLESS QUEST book. You will find yourself returning again and again to experience new paths of excitement. Remember! Only your choices can save the kingdom from the CIRCUS OF FEAR.

Name: Forms and Charts
Description: From the rear cover: For Players and referees to record complete Traveller campaign data... Forms and Charts is the answer: Over twenty blank Traveller forms ready to photocopy or print in quantity, to record character data, subsector and sector mapping, animal encounter tables, starship design, and cargo shipping. Plus, twelve identification cards suitable for a variety of player and non-player characters, and the Imperial calendar.

Name: Dragons of Weng T'Sen
Description: From the back cover: The Emperor has forbidden his subjects to enter the valley. Now mysterious storms and fantastic monsters have begun threatening the nearby countryside. The Emperor's word is law, none of his subjects must enter the valley, but a few chosen heroes from the distant west would be free to enter and brave the dangers in the valley of the Orient's greatest wizard. Discover new and exciting challenges as your characters face the combined forces of nature and magic in 11th century China. Only then, after you've overcome all other obstacle, can you meet the true lords of the elements. DRAGONS OF WENG T'SEN is a fantasy roleplaying module for three to six players from sixth to ninth level. Suitable for use with all popular roleplaying systems including Advanced Dungeons and Dragons

Name: The Haiphong H.A.L.O.
Description: Recon Module 1. Printed in a large, multi-page newsprint format. Publisher's description: "Under OPS 31 (Maritime) and OPS 35 (North Viet Nam) SOG RTs penetrated deep into the heart of North Viet Nam. The Haiphong HALO allows you to recreate some of the most dramatic missions of that war. This module is designed to allow you to run overland or amphibious operations with new insertion and contact systems and tables." "Special terrain generation tables for the Central Highlands and the Coastal Plain allow you to lead your RT overland to the very gates of Hanoi." "New insertion and extraction systems allow you to take SEALs or Marine Force Recon swimmers (SCUBA divers) into Haiphong harbor for recon, sabotage, snatches, assassinations, and rescues."

Name: The Race Forever
Description: Reader summary R.A. Montgomery takes the reader on not just one, but two separate African road races in Choose Your Own Adventure #17. Like other early books in the series such as Inside UFO 54-40, this offering clearly shows the authors experimenting with the new genre.

Name: The Lost Tribe
Description: Reader summary The 23rd Choose Your Own Adventure book is also Louise Munro Foley's first. In this adventure, the hunt for a lost tribe takes you all the way to New Zealand, with plenty of pitfalls lying in wait.

Name: Veterans
Description: From the back of the book: When adventurers want to hire mercenaries, the referee needs a supply of experienced characters for them to choose from. This supplement contains over two hundred characters generated using the rules in Book 4, Mercenary, with resumes for the players to see and a separate section containing information reserved for the referee. The veterans come from all branches of the Army and Marines; tech levels run from 7 to 15.

Name: Sayaret & Track Commander
Description: Recon Supplement 1. Publisher description: "Covers running an Israeli reconnaissance/special operations character against terrorists and conventional troops. Track Commander introduces a roleplaying system for being the crew of an Israeli tank or armored personnel carrier."

Name: Merc: Supplement 1
Description: Publisher blurb: The purpose of Supplement 1 is to add Specialties and Skills, and to clarify existing material. In no way is this Supplement meant to change the present game system. In Book 3 - In Country, besides new specialties and skills, there are restrictions on existing material. In Book 4 - Motor Pool, the vehicle procedure has been intensified. All and any of these changes should be applied to existing characters and campaigns. Since the basic MERC system remains the same, these changes are only to add authenticity, realism, and action to your game.

Name: The Korellian Empire
Description: Star Sector Atlas 12 is a supplement for Space Opera. This 46 page sourcebook on the Korellian Empire includes sections on the history, social institutions, government, civil service, ministries, religion, telepathy, physiology, armed forces, unit organizations, and starforces of the Empire, 2 starsector maps and lists of planets, details of 60 planets, animal encounters, and Korellian military ground and air equipment.

Name: Dungeon Floor Plans 3
Description: Dungeon Floor Plans 3 are an extension of the Dungeon Floor Plans role-playing game aid and give rooftops, boats, features and accessories for quayside, city and village adventures using 25mm figures. Contains 12 coloured sheets of cards with a superimposed square grid as follows: Four sheets representing roof tiles Two sheets representing roof junctions Two sheets representing timber features Two sheets representing boats Two sheets representing stone/dirt outdoor features Each sheet should be cut up by the user using a sharp knife. (From the back of the box)

Name: Doc's Island
Description: From the front cover: After centuries of strife between the forces of Good and Evil, your party, under the leadership of the famed Paladin Athelstan, seeks to recover the Holy Sword, Chrysomer – one of the great Legendary Swords now in the hands of Evil forces. This is the last in the "R" series of AD&D® modules. Each RPGA module has been used at an official Gen Con tournament, and has been designed to provide maximum entertainment. Doc's Island is usable in campaign or tournament play. The original five characters are included in the module, along with character details, historical background, overview maps, and detailed encounter descriptions for the Dungeon Master.

Name: Revolt of the Dwarves
Description: An ENDLESS QUEST Adventure #5 You are Galen, whose family is captured by dwarven warriors revolting against human rule. Can you put a stop to the Revolt of the Dwarves? The paths you take may lead to freedom ... or death. The choices are yours. Only you can decide whether you try to warn the kingdom or to stop the dwarves yourself. Will you travel down the river of dragons, knowing that magical monsters live there? Or will you tackle the dwarves in their own dark caves and try to rescue your parents? No matter what choices you make, adventure and action are yours in ENDLESS QUEST books. You will find yourself returning again and again to experience new paths of excitement. Will YOUR choices successfully prvent the REVOLT OF THE DWARVES?

Name: Brotherhood of the Bolt
Description: form scenario description In this series of adventures, the setting of play is the wilderness near the village of Wentworth, Shaltor Notch, and the mountains to the east that border the Kingsway. Shaltor Notch, of historic and strategic importance, is a narrow pass in the Snowpeak Mountains through which runs the Kingsway, a paved road many centuries old, patrolled and maintained by the King. Spanning the notch is a fortification that has been turned into an inn, and at the foot of the notch on the west side is the ancient Barrow of Shaltor Plain. On Thunder Peak stands the castle of Morlach the Malicious, a member of the Brotherhood of the Bolt, an ancient secret society of necromancers. Morlach is attempting to gain power, either by becoming an undead lord (the ultimate aim of all of the members of the Brotherhood) or by becoming powerful in local mundane matters. He is convinced the way to these goals is by breaking into the Barrow.

Name: Within the Tyrant's Demesne
Description: "A complete campaign module set in the world of Haven" From the back cover: Whose Side Are You On?!! Within the Tyrant's Demesne is a complete campaign module, detailing the inhabitants, shops, and other places of interest in dhar Ankhes. It can be used independently or in conjunction with other Gamelords campaign packs — particularly City of the Sacred Flame, which describes the stronghold of dhar Ankhes traditional nemesis, the barbarian tribesmen of Zal' Akhen. This module utilizes the Thieves' Guild rules system, but is compatible with any major fantasy role-playing system. Within the Tyrant's Demesne contains a detailed city map, two complete introductory scenarios, several additional mini-scenarios and city encounter tables — enough material for months of adventuring. So if it's cloak-and-dagger suspense you're after, then seek no further, effendi — dhar Ankhes is the place your campaign has been looking for!

Name: Underground Kingdom
Description: You are exploring the Underground Kingdom, a fantastic and strange world in the center of the earth. As you stand inside the largest cavern you've ever seen, you are amazed at the dazzling sight before your eyes... (From back cover.)

Name: Hyperspace
Description: Reader summary In the 21st entry in the Choose Your Own Adventure series, the reader-protagonist becomes involved with a batty genius's experiments with hyperspace. Edward Packard continues to play with the genre, with self-referential passages and winks at his readership galore.

Name: The Elixir of Life
Description: From the cover - The Kingdom of Pellham is in danger. Only the return of the great King Llywelyn can solve the realm's problems and satisfy the people. Many arduous tasks must be performed before the great king can be resurrected-tasks YOU have been chosen to undertake. You must solve arcane mysteries and overcome dire perils to accomplish your quest. Are you equal to the challenges you face? The Elixir of Life is the second in a series of RPGA modules concerning the Prophecy of Brie. The module contains two AD&D adventures for 6-10 characters, levels, 4-7. The module was originally run as part of a tournament at the GEN CON XVI Convention. The module contains two wilderness adventures, background information, maps and detailed encounter keys.

Name: TimeLords Second Edition
Description: From the back of the book: "TimeLords. Where you can design and play the most challenging character of them all...yourself. The system lets you use your present stats, or any alternate persona you can think of, the character you are, or the one you would like to be. Thrust through unknown time, space and probability, to go home you must learn to control the awesome forces at your disposal. Incredibly detailed, incredibly accurate, the universal TimeLords system works in any place, time or genre, with full rules on all forms of combat, sections of time and dimension travel, and the ability to adapt to any type of playing or GM'ing style. Players also have the opportunity to join the Time Patrol, creating characters from the ground up, from any period or background, to act as agents of this elite force. In the patrol, you can help preserve the present by foiling plots in the past. Or, just use the TimeLords system as a base for your fantasy, science-fiction or post-holocaust campaigns. The possibilities are endless. There is no place you cannot go. Adventure into forever. Be a TimeLord." Timelords begins at the End of Time. A doomed race, known as "The Designers", gifted in both psionics and sciences set about escaping their fate as the inevitable end of the universe encroached. Eventually, they discover time travel, and use this knowledge to move their entire solar system backwards in time to when the universe is about 15 billion years old. The actual Designers have never been seen by humans. After attempting to create powerful new life forms, they created "Lucifer", a sentient being that led the destruction of the Designer race and caused the dispersion of Designer technology across the universe. The pinnacle of their achievements was the "Matrix", a small time-travel device, the size and shape of a 20-sided die, weighing about 3 pounds. The Matrix could be used for personal time travel, as an energy source, and other things only hinted at by the rule books. It is one of these Matrix devices that the players of the game find to begin their adventure.

Name: Black Opal Eye
Description: From the back of the book: The fear of doom is haunting the fair elven forest. An ancient enemy, dark and sinister, is threatening them once more. Three witches inhabit the ruins of the wizard Elyas' tower, and their evil schemes are now in motion. Two elf maidens have disappeared without trace. None of those sent to explore the witches' tower have returned. The elves' magic is failing, and they have turned to you for help. You must enter the tower, discover its secrets, and defeat the evil of the witches forever. Are you equal to the challenge? Black Opal Eye is a complete D&D Basic adventure for a balanced party of 5-8 adventurers, levels 2-3. It can be played on its own or as a sequel to RPGA Module 1. Black Opal Eye includes background information, maps of the tower and detailed encounter information for the DM.

Name: The Mountain Environment
Description: From the back: The Mountain Environment is a Traveller supplement dealing with the particular situation and problems of travel and survival in rugged terrain. Included are specific rules systems designed to simulate mountaineering and other activities and hazards found in mountain terrain, explanations of equipment available for use in such terrain, and a guide to constructing specific mountain situations, special events and encounters, and adventures. Challenge your players to broaden their horizons and enter The Mountain Environment.

Name: Advanced Laserburn & Aliens
Description: From the front of the book: More comprehensive character development Advanced firing and combat rules & FIVE ALIEN RACES

Name: Most Wanted Volume 1
Description: [From the back of the book.] And now for something completely dangerous! From the files of C.H.E.S.S. headquarters comes a cross-section of the criminal elements; the complete data on 30 super-villains, their powers, origins, psychological profiles and tactics. But have a care, the kid gloves are now off! These people mean serious business. Also included are over 100 full-color counters for use in your V&V campaign, complete with agents, vehicles, thugs, and these villains: Bandit Beekeeper Boogey Man Blizzard Crossfire Dragonhand Ebony Angel Jaws Leo Madame Frigia Marutukku Mirage Mother Superior Motivator Mountain Man Mystico Nightmare Od Psiphon Samhain Shadowjack Simon Shrew Snow Man Spyder Stigmata Tank Gunner Tengu Trickshot Vibron Vindicator

Name: Shadowcastle
Description: From the Face-page: You are Molly. You and Thistle, the little halfling, follow the Good Knight of the Golden Dragon and his soldiers to SHADOWCASTLE in search of your friend and Thistle's brother. From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

Name: Denial of Destiny
Description: From the book text: This mission is designed for the crew of a Constitution Class Starship, possibly the USS Enterprise... The adventure will involve both action aboard a Constitution Class Starship, and on the surface of an unfamiliar, though relatively hospitable planet... This adventure should be undertaken by a fairly large group of players; the recommended minimum number is four.

Name: The Compleat Adventurer
Description: From the back cover: Featuring rules for the Beastmaster, Buccaneer, Bounty Hunter, Gladiator, Harlequin, Knight, Martial Artist, Rogue, Scout, Spy, Swordsman, Warrior, and Witch Hunter as a player character adaptable to most fantasy role-playing games.

Name: Stormbringer Companion
Description: From the back of the book: "Within this book you will find two main sections: Additions and Scenarios. The Additions section contains creatures, heroes (or antagonists), and exotic treasures for use by the Game Master in Stormbringer adventures. The Scenarios section contains six far-ranging adventures ready for immediate play."

Name: The Book of Swords and other Magical Weapons
Description: From the back cover: Inside this book you will find a wide range of interesting weapons to enhance and Fantasy Role-Playing game. Each weapon is beautifully illustrated and described in detail. The Book of Swords will add a note of realism to your game, while helping your players to visualize your world. Adding your own imagination to this valuable playing aid will make your world truly unique.

Name: The Undersea Environment
Description: From the back: The Undersea Environment is a Traveller supplement dealing with the particular problems found in an aquatic environment. Included are specific rules systems designed to simulate underwater activities and hazards, explanations of gear and equipment available for underwater use, and a guide to special events and encounters to aid the referee in constructing underwater encounter tables. Challenge your players to broaden their horizons and enter The Undersea Environment.

Name: The Phantom Submarine
Description: Reader summary The 26th book in the Choose Your Own Adventure series takes the reader back underwater. The protagonist acts as a government agent working to help end skullduggery on, and beneath, the high seas. This time, you're not only armed with quick wits, but also with extra-sensory perception. This is Richard Brightfield's second book in the series.

Name: Exotic Atmospheres
Description: From the back cover: This special supplement provides a more detailed and comprehensive look at exotic, insidious, and corrosive atmospheres. It originally appeared as a pull-out section in Journal of the Travellers' Aid Society (Issue 17).

Name: Operation Peregrine
Description: Space Opera: Operation Peregrine - The Quanchiovt Conspiracy "ASSIGNMENT: BREAK A CRIMINAL SYNDICATE A religious leader is kidnapped and the trail leads the adventurers through a variety of worlds on the track of a syndicate. Criminal masterminds running illegal drugs and operating production facilities on many worlds are involved. These types of people have money and a ruthless nature in covering up their activities. The player characters will find that their foes will stop at nothing, even disrupting the annual Sky Cruiser Race on Grutzom. It all makes for an exciting, multi-part adventure of danger and intrigue. Also included in OPERATION PEREGRINE is a complete quadrant of a star sector. Maps and commercial starship routes accompany the descriptions of the planets in this quadrant. "

Name: Casino Galactica
Description: Casino Galactica is a supplement for Space Opera. This 20 page adventure module outlines a variety of scenarios based in a luxury resort hotel on Arcturus VI in the Terran Sector.

Name: Steve Jackson's Sorcery! (Two Book Set)
Description: From the back of the case Sorcery is the tale of a hero’s quest!.. But it is no ordinary tale because the hero is YOU! You stand before the Cantopani Gate, the last barrier between the civilized kingdom and chaos that is Kakhabad, the dark land at the end of the earth. You are about to embark on a perilous journey - for your mission is to retrieve the Crown of Kings, stolen by an evil war-wizard. You may choose to face your foes using the magical arts of sorcery (The Sorcery Spell Book will assist you) or wielding the power of the sword, but whether it is as a wizard or warrior you will need to keep all your wits about you as you set out on the most fantastic adventure of your life! Packaging: This "Steve Jackson Sorcery" set contains the first books published for the series; Sorcery! Book 1: The Shamutanti Hills and The Sorcery Spell Book.

Name: Margin of Error
Description: From the introduction: Stardate 7931.43. Three hours out from the Epsilon One station on the Klingon frontier. We are hoping to aid that station in examining a strange phenomenon that occurred across the Klingon border three months ago. I have called my department heads to the briefing room to discuss the situation there. User summary: Manuscript copies of the scenario are available for public reading at Tracy and Laura Hickman papers on Star Trek Margin of Error role-playing game; MSS 8180; Tracy and Laura Hickman papers; L. Tom Perry Special Collections; Literary Manuscripts; 1130 Harold B. Lee Library; Brigham Young University; Provo, Utah 84602. The folder contains multiple copies of the manuscript in various stages of development; several are heavily annotated. The version recorded here, at 18-pages, is probably the final development stage in the folder.

Name: Indian Trail
Description: Publisher blurb You live with your tribe in a pueblo village. There has been no rain for a long time and the crops you grow are dying. The only hope for your village is to ask for help from the spirits your people call kachinas. You are told they are very powerful and helpful spirits. They can even change the weather! You have danced and prayed to the kachinas, but now the time has come to go find them. The trail up the mountain will be long and dangerous. Will you bring your friends? Or do you decide to face the wilderness alone?

Name: Horrors of the Haunted Museum
Description: From the back of the book: Chills, Thrills, Screams.... At night when the museum is dark, strange forms haunt the hallways and creatures of evil roam the shadowy rooms. Can you survive an encounter with the Ghost Pirate of the Caribbean? Find out on page 18. What secret does the Living Mummy wish to keep from you? Find out on page 46! Go ahead - see what horrors are on display in every room. In a TWISTAPLOT Book, you decide which path to take and what will happen once you get there. The choice is yours. Enjoy your visit! Artists: Tom Stöerrle - Cover Daivd G. Klein - Inside Illustrations

Name: Escape
Description: Reader summary In the 20th book of the Choose Your Own Adventure series, R.A. Montgomery sends the reader into the near future. The United States are no longer united, having split into three nations, and the reader becomes involved in espionage between these three new adversaries.

Name: The Formula for Trouble
Description: From the back of the book: You're curious. You're thirsty. You can't resist. One gulp, and anything can happen! Turn to page 14, and learn what life is like at the other end of a leash when your dog starts giving you the orders! Help! On page 26, you stare at the mirror and discover that a hairy werewolf is staring back at you! Go ahead! Take a sip of the Formula for Trouble. In a TWISTAPLOT Book, you're the star of the story! You decide which path to take and what will happen once you get there. The choice is yours. Bottoms up!

Name: Wizard's Island
Description: Wizard's Island ... In a time near the end of the Dark Years, when the forces of evil were fighting to desperately to hold back the changing tide brought on by the Legions of the Four Sages, there was a wizard, Master of Mind Magics, who dabbled with the forces of the Thousand Hells until he had relearned the forbidden Black Art, Necromancy. His powers were such that he could create undead. But he was not satisfied with just blaspheming the dead, he wanted also to blaspheme the living. So he chose instead to conduct other more heinous experiments. He sent his undead ships to raid the coasts of Avland and Tryuna for humans, humanoids, and other creatures to use as guinea pigs. The result of his experiments was the Blasphemer. No two Blasphemers were alike, as each combined the features of two or more living things. Satisfied with his experiments, the evil mage was ready to conquer the world. Only the world had changed in the years he had been engrossed in his experiments: The Dark Year were ended and the golden age of Silverdawn had begun The Golden Empire had rapidly risen to a level of power and wealth unheard of since times before the Dark Years. Still, given time, the necromancer realized that he could build great armies of undead and Blasphemers, and once more assert the rightful dominance of evil. But he had no time. The Sages had heard of his evil exploits and so sent one of their number to end his reign of terror. The Sage of Wizardry had spent the past several years in actual battle so that his combat spells were at their strongest. Kazanlan, the necromancer wizard, was no match for the battle-hardened Sage. Thus it was that the evil mage Kazanjian screamed with his final dying breath this curse: "I swear by the name of my birthing, Kazanjian, that I will return. My blasphemous hordes will reassert evil's dominion and will send the Sages to some dark hell!" Kazanjian; the evil mage, is known in most historical accounts simply as The Wizard. Necromancy was often called Black Wizardry in those times ... Now a group of adventurers have decided to explore what is left of his domain, which they assume to be abandoned ...

Name: Dungeon of the Dragon Lord
Description: From the introduction: The conjurer Drakon and his evil apprentice have been wreaking havoc on the countryside. Many parties have entered the dungeon never to return. Your job is to stop this evil magic-user while he is just beginning to grow, for he is, at the moment, only controlling a small number of young dragons. User summary: For 1st - 4th level characters.

Name: Robot World
Description: From the back cover: You are an Interplanetary Spy. Your mission is to track down Dr. Cyberg, computer genius. He is the only person who can help you stop the rebellion on Robot World before it spreads to the rest of the galaxy!

Name: Invasion of the Black Slime and Other Tales of Horror
Description: From the back cover: You are crossing America on your bicycle. . . When you stop to explore and old mining town. Soon you discover that Silverlode has been invaded by a terrible, bubbling black slime that makes animals howl and people hiss and attack each other in the night. You have no choice but to stay and help solve the mystery! But whether you find yourself trapped in a river of oozing black evil in your own hotel room, running for your life from a hideous manlike monster, walking a narrow path over a pit of rattlesnakes, imprisoned as a sacrifice for a mad doctor's experiments, battling billions of attacking insects, or defying the eerie powers of the yellow-eyed people- never fear! If you make the right choice, you might also win a million dollars! So get ready to plunge into adventure. And if you get caught in the black slime, just go back to the beginning and start again. THIS ADVENTURE BELONGS TO YOU! Welcome to the world of Which Way books- Where YOU make things happen.

Name: The Video Avenger
Description: From the back of the book Your dream has come true. The fantastic ME-II computer you won is sitting in your bedroom, just waiting for you to set dozens of video games in motion. You can hardly wait to play... until you find out that the games are real and that, with on touch, you are trapped inside the computer.

Name: Overlord's Handbook
Description: User Summary The Overlord's Handbook includes general advice for the Overlord in running a campaign and takes the time the emphasize the role-playing possibilities open to the Overlord. The Handbook also introduces a variant that would allow players to get together on a regular basis in a way much more reminiscent of classic RPG play than is suggested in the core rules. Several sections expand on the core rules adding wrinkles to: Politics (population moral, alternate taxation, & new ways to handle media); Spying (several new charts are included); War and Battle (war aids for the Overlord, new ship types, cost to build custom ships, etc.); and Trade (rules for computing how many important trade goods are available at any given moment).

Name: Desert Plots: Amazon Mutual #2
Description: From the covers: "A desert mystery campaign plot with two complete dungeons, 5 plot outlines, 13 new monsters, 18 new magic items, and 18 new traps & tricks. AD&D modules & generic plots linking The Efreet Secret Weapon, Adventurer's Life Assurance, The Great Art of Master Ovid & His Good Demons, The Orcan Liberation Front, The Sexual Mores of the Efreeti Queen, and Belworth George - The Paladin of St. Fallwell."

Name: The Bigfoot Mystery
Description: From publisher blurb: While you're camping in the woods your campsite is attacked by something that leaves behind huge footprints.

Name: Isle of Illusion
Description: From the back of the book: You are Licia, secretly trained as a fighter, and your skills are about to be severely tested as you follow the footsteps of your missing brother in his quest to destroy the evil illusionist, Treg, who holds your family's future in his hands. He is Tym, a young adventurer with a frank, open face and the skills of a thief. And then there is Max, a scholarly magic-user, with the eyes of a mystic and the ability to make you feel emotions you've never felt before. Will your fantasy adventure lead you to your first romance, or will the decisions you make carry you into realms from which there is no return? In a HEARTQUEST book, you, the reader, are challenged to PICK A PATH TO ROMANCE AND ADVENTURE.

Name: Crash Landing!
Description: From the back of the book: Choose from over 30 different endings! CRASH LANDING! You gasp in terror as you hear the pilot announce those terrifying words! You can't believe that your free vacation is about to end in flames! Will you become a newspaper statistic? Or will you use your wits to face the danger...and survive! On page 6, you learn how to guide a parachute over perilous mountains and deserts! Deadly piranha fish threaten your surival on page 41! And if you ever get home, what will you do with all the gold you discover on page 52? In a TWISTAPLOT Book, you are the star of the story! You face the danger! Your survival depends on the choices you make and the pages you turn. So fasten your seatbelt. Your flight is now departing.

Name: Cynfelyn, Prince of Prydein
Description: In Cynfelyn, Prince of Prydein characters move up in the world of international intrigue and espionage between neighboring countries. For levels 4-6.

Name: Threat From Nowhere - The Gift
Description: From the introduction: Six days ago you were in the town of Akhmen, resting after successfully freeing Nefru, an orphan ward of the temple of Anubis, from a deadly magical bond. Some of you had just completed training when the high priest of Anubis in Akhmen told you to seek a rapid return to your home city of Thinnes, though he later had no memory of this event.

Name: The Imliv River Valley
Description: From the front cover: Adventure in the Magical Grimbolwood! Caught between two fighting goblin tribes, the gnomes of the Hilberry Hills are unable to deliver their goods. A party of adventurers is needed to brave the horrors of Brimbolwood and save the town of Drimiliv. The Imliv River Valley is an exciting role-playing adventure designed for use with almost any fantasy role-playing system.

Name: The Deeper Dream
Description: Players in The Deeper Dream generate eight attributes (Will, Imagination, Innate Power, Solidity, Strength, Toughness, Balance, and Aim) using 3d6 for each each. Players then distribute 100 percentage points among one to four skills (taking fewer than four skills allows for faster future progression). Most skills are Dreamworld skills, but some are used in the Waking World. To determine success in opposed actions, percentage dice are rolled and compared to the appropriate skill statistic. If no skill applies, a d20 roll may instead be called for to be made against a suitable attribute. In both cases, the die roll must be lower than the skill or attribute to achieve success. One unique aspect of the game concerns death, which is rarely permanent in dreams. Instead, characters may lose the ability to dream for several days or suffer other negative effects.

Name: Rasterblätter
Description: User summaryRasterblätter (graph paper) is a gaming aid for the German Dungeons & Dragons. The subtitle is Spezialpapier zur Konstruktion von Verliesen (speciality paper for dungeon construction) It is a book with 32 one-sided blank graph paper pages (i.e. 16 sheets with a graph paper front and a blank back). The inside covers show advertisements for the Drache magazine (first German Dragon magazine) and the D&D-Bund, a community of D&D players.

Name: The Cavern of Doom
Description: People have been disappearing in the Great Underground Empire. Can Bivotar and Juranda solve this mystery?

Name: Jungle Safari
Description: From publisher blurb: You go on safari with your uncle Stanley and his daughter, your favorite cousin June. During your journey you hope to find a Kawamba, a rare species of ape not seen in many years.

Name: Raid Against the Empire
Description: Publisher's blurb: Melee & Magic game scenario for 6 to 10 people including [Game Controller] and 2 to 3 actors. It requires 4 game sets made from 3 houses/apts. but little work to prepare the sets for play. It involves no monsters and minimal props. It will take 1 to 2 days to play.

Name: Search for the Treasures of Term
Description: From the introduction: We've tried to present scenarios that can be applied with a minimum of fuss to many game systems. Here are some guidelines to smooth over any rough areas you may encounter... ... Rumors abound in the town of Windemere; they fly to and fro as if riding the backs of the swift Belldoves that nest on Heartbreak Mountain. It's difficult at times to tell the truth from falsehood, but when pilgrims from afar start trudging up the circuitous paths of Rising Moon Hill to pay homage to a new find unearthed by a group of clerics, you know there's more to the hearsay than imagination.

Name: Dream Trips
Description: From publisher blurb: You go to sleep and have dreams.

Name: The Accursed Castle
Description: Publisher's blurb: This location was something I drew up shortly after seeing The Last Unicorn for the first time. I recall really liking the idea of the old king haunted by his past, and drew a lot of inspiration from the concept of a seaside castle fallen under a Wuthering Heights-esque pall of doom and gloom. So, moved by the Muse, I immediately set to work with my mechanical pencil, compass, and graph paper. The result is The Accursed Castle, an immense structure whose foundations are carved deep into the rocky cliffs of The Cold Sea, and whose handful of spires reach high into the gray sky above.
